title: Executive Assistant (Operations & Development-Real Estate)
description:
About Us

We are a premier U.S.-based real estate development and home building group with a heavy footprint in the San Francisco Bay Area. Our portfolio focuses on high-quality residential projects, ranging from ADUs and single-family homes to large-scale community developments. We value speed, clarity, and excellence. We are seeking a high-performance Executive Assistant who thrives in a fast-moving, execution-heavy environment.



Position Summary

The Executive Assistant (EA) will directly support the VP of Development / Chief Asset Management Officer with daily communications, scheduling, and rigorous execution follow-through. You will act as an operational partner, ensuring that land acquisition, project entitlement, and permitting tasks move forward without friction. This role requires exceptional organizational skills and a strong background in real estate development or architectural operations.



Key Responsibilities

1. Communication & Stakeholder Triage

- Manage and filter high-volume inboxes; draft concise, professional replies to city officials, architects, and contractors.

- Track correspondence with partners, consultants, and clients to ensure zero communication backlog.

- Maintain a professional tone in all high-stakes external communications.



2. Task & Project Management

- Maintain and update complex project trackers in Lark, Google Sheets, or Salesforce.

- Proactively follow up with internal teams and external consultants to ensure project milestones (permitting, site visits, and escrow) stay on schedule.

- Help implement and manage CRM or task automation systems to streamline project workflows.



3. Scheduling & Cross-Border Coordination

- Manage calendars across multiple entities and time zones (U.S.–Asia).

- Schedule internal team syncs, consultant meetings, site visits, and board meetings.

- Prepare detailed agendas and distribute "Action-Item" recap notes with a clear reminder system for all parties involved.



4. Document & Research Support

- Organize and maintain digital files (blueprints, permits, and reports) in Dropbox/Box.

- Assist in preparing project presentations, meeting briefs, and development progress summaries.

- Support real estate development research, including zoning analysis and city-specific ordinance tracking.



Qualifications

- Experience: Minimum 2+ years supporting senior executives in Real Estate Development, Architectural Design, Home Building or related fields.

- Industry Knowledge: Understanding of residential project flow (LOIs, pro formas, and permitting timelines).

- Tech Stack: High proficiency in Lark, Google Workspace,and Dropbox, Microsoft Suites, Canva and project management softwares.

- Language: Excellent written and spoken English

- Presence: Sharp, organized, detail-oriented, discreet, and proactive.



Compensation & Structure

- Full-time remote role with partial Pacific Time overlap.

- Competitive base salary/retainer + performance bonuses.

- Long-term growth potential within the investment and operations leadership track.



Submit your resume in a pdf format.

title: Landscape Architect
description:
Position Summary

We are seeking a talented and detail-oriented Landscape Architect to join our remote design team. This role is responsible for developing high-quality landscape plans for residential projects in California while ensuring seamless coordination with our design group. The ideal candidate will have expertise in landscape design, site planning, and sustainability, with strong communication and collaboration skills for remote teamwork.



Key Responsibilities

- Design landscape plans for residential projects in California, ensuring aesthetic appeal and functionality.

- Create planting plans, hardscape layouts, irrigation designs, and outdoor living concepts tailored to client needs.

- Research and apply California-specific zoning, environmental, and planting regulations to design solutions.

- Develop technical drawings, site plans, and 3D renderings using industry-standard software.

- Coordinate closely with the design team, project managers, and consultants to align landscaping with overall project vision.

- Assist in concept presentations, material specifications, and design revisions based on client feedback.

- Stay informed on sustainable landscape practices, native plant selections, and water-efficient designs.

- Manage multiple projects while ensuring timely delivery of high-quality landscape plans.



Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Landscape Architecture, Architecture, or a related field.

- Proven experience in residential landscape design and remote collaboration.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp, Lumion, Adobe Creative Suite, and other landscape design tools.

- Strong understanding of California climate, planting guidelines, and sustainability principles.

- Excellent communication and coordination skills for working remotely with a U.S.-based team.

- Ability to manage time effectively and deliver quality work within project deadlines.

- Knowledge of irrigation systems, hardscape materials, and outdoor lighting is a plus.





Kindly send your CV/Resume and Portfolio and make sure it is accessible. Goodluck!

title: Operations Support Center Manager
description:
We are hiring an experienced Operations Support Center Manager based in the Philippines to oversee and improve a team responsible for high-volume coordination, scheduling, and support operations for a US-based client.



This role manages a team of approximately 10 offshore staff who handle onboarding coordination, scheduling, field communication, and issue resolution.



We are looking for a manager who is strong in both people leadership and process improvement—someone who can ensure execution runs smoothly while continuously improving efficiency and quality.



This is a full-time role working US hours.



Key Responsibilities

Team Management

Manage and lead a team of ~10 Operations Support Specialists

Monitor performance, productivity, and quality of work

Provide coaching, feedback, and accountability

Handle performance issues and escalate when necessary

Ensure tea
ets daily and weekly operational targets

Operations Oversight

Oversee day-to-day execution of scheduling, onboarding support, and field communication

Ensure tasks are completed accurately and on time

Maintain consistency across team workflows and outputs

Identify gaps, delays, or breakdowns in execution

Process Improvement

Identify inefficiencies in current workflows and suggest improvements

Improve team productivity, turnaround times, and accuracy

Standardize processes and ensure best practices are followed

Work with leadership to refine operational processes over time

Reporting & Communication

Track key performance metrics (productivity, response times, completion rates)

Provide regular updates to leadership

Ensure clear communication between tea
mbers and stakeholders

Maintain organized documentation of processes and activities

Requirements

3+ years experience in operations, call center, or support team management

Experience managing teams of 5–10+ people

Strong leadership and coaching skills

Strong organizational and problem-solving skills

Ability to manage high-volume, detail-oriented work environments

Strong written and verbal English communication skills

Comfortable working US Central Time hours

Preferred Experience (Nice to Have)

Experience managing scheduling or coordination teams

Experience in BPO, staffing, or operations support environments

Experience improving workflows or operational processes

Experience using Google Workspace (Gmail, Sheets, Docs, Drive)

Tools

Google Workspace (Gmail, Sheets, Docs, Drive)

Compensation

Competitive salary based on experience (Philippines-based)

Full-time, long-term opportunity

Growth potential as operations scale



What We’re Looking For



This is not just a supervisory role.



We are looking for someone who:



Takes ownership of team performance

Identifies problems and improves them

Brings structure and consistency to operations

Can manage both people and execution effectively

To Apply



Please include:



A summary of your experience managing teams

The size of teams you have managed

An example of a process improvement you implemented

The word “Neverland” in your application to confirm attention to detail



Only applicants who follow instructions will be considered.

title: UK Company Hiring: US Cold Callers
description:
Hiring  Cold callers to Help Build Local Business In the USA

 Base pay $600 per month (after two week training) plus commissions OTE each month $1000-$1500

Company: Start Over Movement Holdings Ltd (Founded by Sir Marco Robinson)

Location: Remote - 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM Philippines Time (8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Mississippi Central Time)

Type: Full-time Independent Contractor

---

The Story Behind Start Over

Sir Marco Robinson is a UK entrepreneur, author, and TV personality who has spent decades helping people rebuild their lives and businesses. He founded Start Over Movement with a simple idea: local business owners should support each other, not compete for the same scraps.

Every Start Over chapter is a community of local business owners who:

Refer clients to each other, generating real revenue

Learn marketing and sales directly from Sir Marco

Get a dedicated commission-only closer assigned to their business

Meet monthly with an exclusive group (only one business per industry per chapter)

It is not networking. It is a referral engine with a mentor attached. Members pay £1,500 (UK) or $1,500 (US and Canada) per year to be part of it. And the model works - we already have active chapters in Laguna Beach, Tampa Bay, New York, London and Toronto. Mississippi is next.

---

Why We Need You

We are looking for 3 cold callers each week to join the team now. We need in total another 180 cold callers within 3 months so this team and the level of recruitment will grow quickly to help launch and fill  up a weekly webinar  the Mississippi chapter. This is part of a broader buildout of 100+ cold callers across all our territories.



The business owners we call are not hostile. They are overwhelmed, under-marketed, and tired of networking groups that do not deliver. When you explain what Start Over actually does - referrals, mentorship, exclusive local representation - the good ones lean in.

closers then to attend a webinar designed to close them for us





The 14-Day Trial

We do not rush this. Your first 14 days are a paid trial:

$15/day base, paid daily via Wise (for verified work days)

Full commission on every sale you close during the trial

Day 1: Onboarding, script training, shadowing, pass the roleplay by end of day

Day 2 onwards: Full production with daily minimums

Live coaching from the Cold Caller Manager throughout

Roleplay check must be passed on Day 1 before going on real calls Day 2

---

What Makes This Role Different

1. You are not hustling a bad offer. Start Over is real. Sir Marco is real. Members are paying, showing up, and referring each other. You are selling something that delivers.

2. You are part of a real team. We are building out 100+ closers across all territories, but we do it properly. You are not one of thousands dialling into a chaotic floor. You get proper coaching, real territory, clear structure, and a Cold Caller Manager who knows your name and your numbers.

3. You have a growth path. Mississippi is the start. As we launch new chapters, top performers get first pick. Build your book with us and grow as we grow.

4. You get real support. Cold Caller Manager in twice-weekly coaching sessions. Direct line to Marco Fernandes (Sales Director, 39 years of sales experience). Proven script refined across thousands of calls. Calendar booking, leads, and territory handed to you.

---

The Daily Reality

Let us be honest about what the day looks like:

9:00 PM - 6:00 AM Philippines Time (8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Mississippi Central Time) - yes, graveyard

Monday to Friday, five nights a week

300 dials or 120 connects minimum per day from Day 2 (whichever you hit first)

10+ event bookings per day (prospects committed to attend the next Start Over event), OR 1+ direct sale, OR 15+ strong Zoom follow-ups booked

Log every call in real time on the shared tracker

End-of-day report to the Cold Caller Manager

Twice weekly meetings (Tuesdays and Fridays typically)

It is not light work. Cold calling is hard. Graveyard is harder. But it is clear work with clear outcomes, and for the right person it pays better than almost anything else a Filipino remote worker can do.

---

We Are Looking For Someone Who

Must have:

2+ years phone sales or closing experience

OnlineJobs.ph ID Proof 60+ verified on your profile

NBI clearance (or willingness to obtain within 2 weeks of hire)

Strong English, clear accent, professional tone

Real experience closing on the call (not just setting appointments)

Can commit to 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM PHT graveyard shift, Monday to Friday

Own Zoom Pro account

Reliable internet (25+ Mbps - speed test screenshot required with application), quiet space, good headset

Will thrive if you:

Have sold coaching, consulting, BNI, networking memberships, or community products before

Care about what you sell, not just about commission

Take coaching well

Can handle "no" all day without losing energy

Want a long-term role, not a quick gig

---

Honest Caveats

The first chapter in every territory is the hardest. List building, market education, all of it.

Your first week, you may not close. That is normal. Week 2 is where the trial gate lives.

Mississippi is a smaller market than our other chapters. That means you can cover the whole state, but you will also work the list harder.

Overnight shifts are tough. Please do not apply if you cannot genuinely commit to the schedule long-term.

---

Verification and Accountability

We use:

TimeProof (you install on your machine, screenshots and activity tracking)

GoHighLevel / Ringover (dial tracking, call recordings, connect verification)

Shared call tracker (real-time logging)

Random call audits (5+ recorded calls per contractor per day reviewed)

This is not surveillance for its own sake. It is so that:

We know you are working the hours you claim

You are protected if we ever question your output - the data has your back

Coaching can be real, because we can listen to your actual calls

We pay for work verified by these systems. Discrepancies get paid only to the verified level. Clear and fair.

Zero tolerance for fraud: Fabricated connects, mouse jigglers or other activity simulators, fake call logs, or dials to deliberately invalid numbers = immediate termination. Pay for fabricated work will be withheld or clawed back. This is the only thing we are strict about. Real closers never have an issue with this.

---

Termination

Independent contractor. Either party can end the agreement at any time, with or without cause, without notice. We pay for all hours worked and commissions earned up to that point.

---

Group Interview - MONDAY

We are hosting a group interview tomorrow (Monday) at 7pm Manila time (12pm UK, 7am Mississippi).





Zoom link:





 









This is how we meet candidates at scale and make fast decisions. Come ready with:

Webcam and microphone working

Professional background and attire

Ready to do a short live roleplay on a cold call

Questions about the role

If you cannot attend live, send your application anyway and we will include you in the next round.

---

How To Apply

Message us through OnlineJobs.ph with:

Name and location

Internet speed test screenshot (from speedtest.net - must show 25+ Mbps)

ID Proof score (60+ required) and NBI clearance status



Confirmation that you can work 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM PHT graveyard shift consistently

No voice recording = we cannot review you. We hire on voice, not paper, for phone sales roles.

---

Start Over Movement. Building real communities. Paying real money. Looking for Three STRONG cold calllers NOW for Mississippi

We look forward to meeting you tomorrow.

title: Healthcare Technical Support
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/17b2a6df-a72d-480e-8c91-daa3830ac000



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

As a Technical Product Support Specialist, your primary responsibility is to provide patients with exceptional technical support for our products and services. You will daily engage non-tech-savvy patients on technical phone calls. Most of our patients are older adults located in the USA. These calls involve guiding patients through using their Android tablet and verifying it in the backend through Samsung Knox, ensuring that RPM devices are connected and transmitting accurate data, and assisting them in joining their appointments through our tablet via Zoom. Occasionally, patients may require additional help during their time in our program. In some instances, verifying data through Azure (Device Analytics and CosmosDB) may be necessary.



Furthermore, you will provide technical support to our clinical team, and ensure they are prepared for their virtual appointments. They may also require assistance using Athenahealth, such as modifying visits and checking in/out. You will handle general general technical support duties, which demand a solid understanding of our tech stack and products (i.e., Android Tablet, Zoo
etings and phone, Google Workspace). You should be capable of providing basic troubleshooting steps and assistance to remove obstacles for clinicians/support staff in their work, and are expected to be able to have on-call availability for urgent situations where patient care is affected.



Collaborating closely with our Engineering Team is also an essential aspect of your role to effectively escalate issues requiring broader coordination and resolution. GitLab and Azure DevOps are the primary ticketing systems used for filing issues and requests. Continuous follow-ups and monitoring are expected until the issue is resolved end-to-end. In addition, you are expected to be able to present and take notes on meetings about issues encountered in Tech.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable navigating ambiguity due to the ever-evolving nature of our startup. To excel in this role, excellent problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills are essential.



A Technical Product Support Specialist is expected to:

• Maintain a reliable, high-speed internet connection

• Handle Tech Support requests filed by clinicians or patients from end to end

• Manage and organize various project tools (e.g., GitLab, AzureDevOps, spreadsheets, bug trackers, documents)

• Conduct Tech Calls for non-tech-savvy patients (mainly older adults) in the USA

• Guide patients through Android tablet usage and confirm settings through Samsung Knox

• Work with RPM devices to ensure they are connected and transmitting accurate data

• Collaborate with Engineering Team and Program Managers to escalate and resolve issues

• Assist clinicians and company support staff with technical issues

• Run queries and check analytics using Azure’s CosmosDB or Device Analytics

• Become extremely familiar with our proprietary app, tablets, RPM devices, and backend systems.

• Support clinical team with equipment setup and checking applied security policies

• Present and take notes on Technical meetings, specifically on issues encountered and plans to resolve them.



Qualifications and Experience

• Prior experience in a customer support role, such as Technical Support Specialist or Support Engineer

• Expertise in Android Tablets, Samsung Knox Suite, and Knox Manage

• Proficiency in managing Zoo
etings and Zoom Phone

• Familiarity with medical devices (e.g., Blood Pressure monitor, Pulse Oximeter)

• Knowledge of Athenahealth preferred

• Experience using issue tracking software and remote support tools (e.g., Gitlab, Azure DevOps) is a plus

• Understanding of technology systems, particularly Google Workspace

• Positive, enthusiastic attitude and excellent English language skills (C2 level)

• Skilled in technical debugging techniques (e.g., analyzing metrics and logs on Azure)

• Strong written and verbal communication, problem-solving skills, and detail-oriented

• Ability to manage multiple priorities effectively

• Healthcare setting experience advantageous



Type of Role

Full-time: 40 hours a week (with a possible shifting schedule including weekends)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage.

Level: Mid-Career, 3-5 years relevant work experience

Location: Remote

title: General Program Manager
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/4b610b44-ac59-43da-a514-ca69e2cce9c3



We are seeking a dynamic and talented General Program Manager to join our team. This role is perfect for a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare.



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We're seeking a super smart, hungry, and resilient individual who is passionate about technology and healthcare to join us as a Program Manager. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and brings a strong organizational mindset to complex projects. You will be responsible for overseeing multiple complex programs across various departments to ensure successful delivery within scope, timeline, and budget. This role requires a self-motivated individual who can work independently and adapt to the dynamic nature of a growing startup in the healthcare technology space.



Responsibilities

- Impact the strategy and execution for AnsibleHealth's general operations, clinical operations, and technology initiatives, working closely with cross-functional teams.

- Work independently on projects through the full life cycle, overseeing clinical, operational, and technology aspects.

- Develop and manage program plans, schedules, budgets, and resource allocation to ensure successful delivery.

- Define program scope, objectives, and success criteria in collaboration with stakeholders and cross-functional teams.

- Establish program governance structures and processes, including risk management, issue management, and change management.

- Monitor and report on program progress, status, and performance to stakeholders and executive leadership.

- Ensure effective communication and alignment with stakeholders at all levels.

- Continuously identify and mitigate program risks and issues.

- Facilitate program-related meetings and workshops.

- Develop and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders to drive program success and identify growth opportunities.

- Bring a continuous improvement mindset to our operations to ensure we're ready to scale.

- Anticipate issues, propose solutions, manage dependencies, and identify areas for tool/process improvement.

- Collaborate with and steer internal teams, including Marketing, Engineering, Program Management, and Clinical Operations.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable to be in a constantly changing role with a high level of ambiguity - as we are a fast-moving startup operating in an ever-evolving environment. He or she should have excellent analytical, communication and management skills to succeed in this role.



Education

Bachelor's degree from one of the top five universities in the Philippines.



Required Qualifications and competencies

- Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; 0-5 years of relevant work experience is an advantage but not required.

- Demonstrated passion for technology and ability to quickly adapt to new digital tools and platforms.

- Exceptional organizational skills.

- Resilient mindset with the ability to bounce back from challenges and thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

- Keen interest in healthcare and improving patient outcomes; prior healthcare experience is a plus but not mandatory.

- High intellectual curiosity and a hunger for personal and professional development.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically and creatively.

- Proficiency in Google Workspace and willingness to learn new software tools as needed.

- Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.

- Adaptable and comfortable with ambiguity in a startup environment.

- Eagerness to take on leadership roles and initiative to drive projects forward.

- Ability to influence and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.

- Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality work.

- Enthusiasm for continuous learning and process improvement.



The ideal candidate is a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare. We're looking for someone who can bring fresh perspectives, adapt quickly to new challenges, and contribute to our innovative and fast-paced work environment.



Job Details

Full-time: Full-time: 40 hours a week

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work US hours - EST/EDT)

title: Freight Billing Specialist
description:
GH Logistics is a growing freight brokerage and asset based logistics company specializing in dry van and refrigerated freight. Built on disciplined systems, structured SOPs, and financial accuracy, we operate with a strong focus on execution, accountability, and real-time visibility.



As we scale nationally, we are hiring a Freight Billing Specialist to serve as a critical financial control point across both load validation and carrier invoicing workflows, ensuring loads and invoices are accurate, fully documented, dispute-free, and ready for Finance and factoring.



Role Overview

You will act as the final gatekeeper before loads and invoices move to Finance and factoring, ensuring every load is fully validated first, then properly invoiced and submitted. This role requires flexibility, ownership, and the ability to operate across both functions without compromising accuracy or SOP compliance.



What You’ll Be Doing



Load Validation

• Validate all loads before final status (Completed, Cancelled, or TONU)

• Ensure every load reaches complete status (fully documented, financially accurate, dispute-free)

• Review and correct load statuses in TMS

• Enforce complete documentation (BOL, POD, white slips, rate confirmations, accessorials)

• Upload and verify documents in TMS and ELD systems

• Confirm financial accuracy (rates, TONU logic, FSC, accessorials, fines)

• Own and investigate carrier disputes before escalation

• Apply correct TONU vs Cancel determinations

• Maintain structured, audit ready notes on every load

• Prevent incomplete or inaccurate loads from reaching Finance

• Prepare validated loads for invoicing and factoring submission



Carrier Invoicing

• Upload and process carrier invoices accurately in TMS

• Verify invoice amounts against rate confirmations and approved charges

• Ensure all required documents are complete (POD, BOL, detention, lumper, etc.)

• Request and follow up on missing or incomplete invoices

• Coordinate with carriers and factoring companies for documentation

• Identify discrepancies and escalate with proper support

• Submit validated loads to factoring companies per requirements

• Resolve Held, Rejected, and Denied invoices

• Maintain structured, audit-ready invoice records

• Prevent incomplete or unsupported invoices from reaching Finance



What Success Looks Like

• 95%+ of loads reach Finance without rework

• 0 missing documents (POD, white slips, etc.) at billing stage

• 0 financial discrepancies identified by Finance

• All loads and invoices are fully audit-ready

• Minimal disputes due to documentation or validation errors

• Loads and invoices processed within 24–48 hours

• Factoring submissions meet compliance standards



Required Skills & Qualifications

• Experience in US freight brokerage, load auditing, QA, carrier billing, or operations

• Strong understanding of dry van & refrigerated freight

• Knowledge of TONU, rate confirmations, accessorials, and FSC

• Familiarity with TMS, ELD, and tracking systems

• Experience with factoring companies (preferred)

• Strong documentation discipline and audit mindset

• High attention to financial accuracy and detail

• Ability to enforce SOPs without shortcuts

• Strong written and verbal English communication

• Minimum 45–50 WPM typing speed

• Comfortable working in a structured, accountability-driven environment

• Ability to shift priorities between validation and invoicing workflows



Work Setup & Schedule

• Full Time | 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST (San Antonio time)

• Must work U.S. business hours

• Collaborative Zoom-based work environment (camera on)

• Time tracking tools used for accountability



Why Join GH Logistics?

• Independent Contractor Setup

• $75 monthly insurance stipend (eligibility begins 30 days after hire)

• SOP-driven operational systems

• High impact financial control role across two critical workflows

• Direct involvement in revenue protection and billing accuracy

• Performance based growth opportunities

• Structured training and certification path



If you take pride in closing operational loops, enforcing standards, and ensuring financial accuracy from validation to invoicing, this role is designed for you.



Apply here:

title: Dispatch Operations Specialist
description:
AJG Transport (GH Logistics Affiliate) is a US based asset carrier operating 150+ company trucks on dedicated government contract freight. We run 365 days a year on consistent, contracted routes across 48 states. We're scaling fast with new markets, new lanes, new teams, and we need operators who can keep up.



This isn't a BPO. This isn't a call center. You'll be embedded in a real U.S. logistics operation working alongside American dispatchers, drivers, and carriers on live freight.



Role Overview

You're an operator working directly alongside our dispatch and freight management team, handling the higher complexity operational work that keeps our network running. Think of this as the role that handles what most people can't, the exceptions, the escalations, the details that make or break a load.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Monitor and manage active loads across our network. Tracking, status updates, carrier and driver communication, and exception handling. You own the load from pickup to delivery.

• Verify data accuracy, documentation completeness, and process compliance across active loads. Catch problems before they become expensive.

• Conduct check calls, appointment scheduling, detention tracking, rate confirmations. You're the voice on the phone making sure freight moves on time.

• Respond to driver inquiries, route them to the right internal team, document issues. Drivers call with problems and you solve them or escalate them fast.

• Ensure accurate data on TMS for Loads, rates, stops, and documents. Everything in our systems needs to be accurate. You're the last line of defense before bad data creates bad decisions.

• Identify recurring issues, flag inefficiencies, and recommend fixes. We don't want someone who just follows a checklist. We want someone who makes the checklist better.

• Report daily and weekly ops summaries for US leadership.

• Know what you can solve vs. what needs to go up the chain, and route it fast. No sitting on problems.



Who We’re Looking For

• 2+ years experience in freight brokerage, dispatch, or logistics operations. NOT GENERAL VA WORK. We need someone who already knows what a BOL is, what detention means, and how a load moves from quote to delivery.

• Hands on experience with TMS platforms such as McLeod, Aljex, Turvo, or similar. If you've dispatched loads in a real system, we want to talk to you.

• Familiarity with load boards such as DAT, Truckstop, Highway or similar. You've sourced carriers or managed freight on these platforms before.

• Strong written and verbal English. You'll be on calls and
s with US based drivers, carriers, and leadership daily. This is non negotiable.

• Night shift in PH availability — You'll work U.S. business hours. CST timezone alignment preferred (example: 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST/CDT = 8:00 PM – 6:00 AM PHT). 

• Self-directed. You don't wait to be told what to do. You see the problem, you fix it, you report it. If you need someone holding your hand, this isn't the role.



Schedule & Work Setup

• Full time permanent remote role

• Direct Hire to a US Company (not agency or BPO)

• Current needed shifts: 7 AM – 5 PM CDT | 3 PM - 1 AM CDT | 11 PM - 9 AM CDT (San Antonio time)

• Reliable home office setup and back up internet and power stations

• Zoom presence required (camera on throughout shift)

• Possible Hubstaff time tracking during shifts, but is not currently in use.



What You'll Get

• $7/hr USD. Paid consistently biweekly. No delayed payments and no excuses.

• Independent Contractor Setup

• $75 monthly insurance stipend (eligibility begins 30 days after hire)

• Stable & long-term position. This is not project-based nor seasonal. We're building a team and we need people who want to build with us.

• Growth path. 90 day performance review with opportunity to advance into senior operations roles as we scale. We're growing fast and the people who prove themselves early get the best opportunities.

• Direct access to U.S. executive leadership. You're not buried in a call center reporting to a middle manager in Manila. You work directly with our operations team in Texas and Tennessee.

• Real operations work. Live freight, real drivers, real carriers, real money on the line. This isn't data entry with a fancy title.



Who Thrives Here

• People who've worked in a U.S. freight brokerage or 3PL and want something more stable

• Former dispatchers or carrier reps who are tired of BPO environments

• Operators who take ownership. You don't clock in and zone out. You run your loads like they are your business.

• People who want to grow into leadership but need the right company to do it in.



Who This Isn't For

• General VAs looking for easy remote work. This is real logistics and not admin tasks.

• People who need constant supervision or step by step instructions for every task

• Anyone who can't commit to US shift hours consistently

• Candidates without actual freight/logistics experience. We don't have time to teach the basics.



How to Apply:

title: Track and Trace Assistant
description:
GH Logistics is a high performance freight brokerage and asset based logistics company specializing in dry van and reefer freight. We move quickly, but we move with structure. Our operation is built on real time visibility, disciplined execution, and clear communication. We take pride in keeping freight organized, on track, and moving cleanly from pickup to delivery. If you’re someone who enjoys staying engaged, solving live challenges, and being part of a fast moving operations team, you’ll feel right at home here.



Role Overview

As a Track & Trace Assistant, you serve as part of the operational backbone, ensuring shipments are accurately monitored, risks are identified early, and critical information reaches the right people at the right time. if you thrive behind the scenes keeping operations running smoothly, this role is an excellent fit.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Updating assigned sections of the Tracking Dashboard with accurate ETAs, locations, mileage, and notes

• Communicating with carriers via
 and internal channels to gather updates or resolve missing information

• Verifying that carriers are properly assigned, accepted, and actively tracking in required platforms

• Monitoring multiple communication channels to prevent missed updates

• Proactively flagging delays, risks, and irregularities, and escalating when necessary

• Coordinating with the Booking Team to confirm ETAs for time sensitive shipments

• Ensuring clean shift handoffs through verbal (Zoom) and written updates

• Follows instructions consistently and executes with precision

• Team oriented, coachable, and open to feedback without ego



What Success Looks Like

• Assigned tracking sections are consistently accurate and up to date

• Scheduled updates are sent on time

• Critical shipments are monitored closely and handled with urgency from start to finish

• Carrier tracking and acceptance issues are caught early

• Issues are communicated clearly, both verbally and in writing

• Shift handoffs are smooth, complete, and well documented



Who We’re Looking For

• Has experience in US logistics, freight brokerage, or track & trace

• Familiar with US geography and US logistics trucking operations (a plus)

• Highly organized and comfortable working with multiple spreadsheets, dashboards, and group chats

• Understands urgency and prioritization in a live freight environment

• Communicates clearly and professionally via chat and

• Can monitor multiple shipments at once without losing accuracy

• Pays strong attention to detail and uses sound judgment when issues arise

• Follows instructions consistently and values clean processes

• Works well with a team and is open to feedback and coaching. Collaborates without resistance or ego



Why Join GH Logistics?

• Independent Contractor Setup

• $75 monthly insurance stipend (eligibility begins 30 days after hire)

• Structured systems and clear expectations

• Hands on leadership and direct feedback

• Defined KPIs and measurable growth

• Opportunity to grow within a scaling freight brokerage

• Performance based advancement



Schedule & Work Setup

• Full Time Remote Role| Direct Hire to US Company

• Current Shift Availability: 7 AM - 5 PM CDT & 11 PM - 9 AM CDT

• Other shifts (only if assigned): 3 PM - 1 AM CDT

• Zoom presence (with camera open and unmuted audio) required

• Time tracking software used for accountability and performance support



This role is built for someone who genuinely enjoys following freight in motion, monitoring shipments, confirming updates, and making sure deliveries stay on track. If you like staying proactive, spotting risks before they become problems, and keeping operations running smoothly behind the scenes, you’ll fit right in.

mber,

mber(s)

mber(s))

title: Video Editor
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



Alloy is a growth partner for association management companies across the U.S. We’re looking for a

Video Production Specialist to join our team and produce high-volume, polished video content

across a range of formats—from marketing campaigns to educational content to social media.

You’ll be our go-to person for all things video. That means you should be just as comfortable editing

a talking-head interview as you are assembling a complex piece with mixed assets like motion

graphics, screen recordings, and slide-based visuals. Because we work with multiple clients across

different industries and brand voices, you’ll need to adapt your style and creative approach to match

each client’s tone, aesthetic, and audience.



This is not a “wait for footage” role. Our clients rarely have existing video assets, so you’ll need to be

a resourceful creator who can build compelling content from scratch using stock footage, AI-

generated media, motion graphics, and original assets. You’ll work alongside strategists and account

managers who handle ideation and client relationships, but you’ll have significant creative ownership

over how content comes to life.



WHAT YOU’LL BE WORKING ON

   • Marketing videos — promotional content, brand storytelling, product explainers, and

campaign assets

   • Talking-head videos — clean, professional edits with dynamic pacing, lower thirds, b-roll

cutaways, and branded overlays

   • Webinar and presentation edits — transforming raw webinar recordings and slide decks

into polished, engaging content

   • E-course and learning content — lesson videos for online courses, including multi-format

edits that blend talking head, slides, screen captures, and graphics

   • Social media clips — short-form vertical and horizontal cuts optimized for Instagram,

TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn

   • Mixed-asset production — assembling final videos from a variety of source materials

including talking-head footage, motion graphics, static graphics, and audio tracks

   • Stock footage and AI-generated content — sourcing, curating, and creatively repurposing

stock footage and AI-generated visuals to build narratives when original footage is

unavailable

   • Template and asset library management — maintaining organized project files, branded

templates, and reusable asset libraries to support high-volume output without sacrificing

quality



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR



Must-Have

   • Proven experience in video production and post-production, with a portfolio that

demonstrates range across multiple video formats



Adobe Creative Suite proficiency:



– Premiere Pro — your primary editing environment

– After Effects — for motion graphics, compositing, and visual polish

– Photoshop — for asset creation, thumbnail design, and graphic prep

– Audition — for audio cleanup, mixing, and mastering



AI video tool fluency — you actively use AI-powered tools (e.g., Runway, Descript, HeyGen,

ElevenLabs, Opus Clip, CapCut, Pika, or similar) to accelerate production without sacrificing quality.

This is a major requirement—we want someone who is already integrating AI into their workflow, not

just curious about it.



   • Familiarity with AI-powered research and scripting workflows (e.g., Google NotebookLM,

Claude, ChatGPT) for content development and scripting support

   • Demonstrated ability to build polished video content from stock footage, screen recordings,

and limited source material—you don’t wait for perfect assets to start creating

   • Strong eye for quality—every deliverable should feel polished and professional, whether it’s

a 15-second social clip or a 45-minute course module

   • Ability to work with mixed media—comfortable combining talking-head footage, motion

graphics, static slides, screen recordings, and audio into cohesive final products

   • Strong eye for pacing, color grading, typography, and sound design

   • Comfortable working across formats: horizontal (YouTube/web), vertical

(Reels/TikTok/Shorts), and square (social)

   • Reliable communication and availability—responsive, proactive, and comfortable working

asynchronously across time zones



Nice-to-Have

   • Animation skills (2D motion design, character animation, kinetic typography, Lottie, etc.)

   • Experience with color grading and advanced audio design

   • Familiarity with YouTube optimization (thumbnails, chapters, end screens)

   • Experience producing content for marketing funnels or growth campaigns

   • Experience in an agency or white-label environment serving multiple brands

   • Familiarity with the HOA, property management, or real estate industry

   • Basic knowledge of SEO and social media distribution strategies for video content

   • Experience with DaVinci Resolve or other editing platforms

   • Illustration, graphic design, or 3D motion skills

   • Ability to work from creative briefs with minimal hand-holding



WHAT WE OFFER

   • Fully remote position with flexible scheduling

   • The opportunity to shape a growing creative function from the ground up

   • A collaborative, low-ego team that values speed, quality, and resourcefulness

   • Exposure to a wide variety of client industries and creative challenges

   • Room to grow as our video capabilities expand across the client portfolio



HOW TO APPLY

Please submit:



1. Your portfolio or showreel — we want to see range. Include examples of talking-head

edits, mixed-asset videos, social clips, and any course/webinar content you’ve produced.

Bonus points for work where you built something compelling without much source material.

2. A brief note on your AI workflow — tell us which AI tools you use, how you use them, and

how they’ve improved your speed or output quality.

3. Your availability and preferred working hours.

We value speed, quality, and creative problem-solving. If you’re someone who’s constantly leveling

up your toolkit and can deliver professional-grade video content efficiently, we’d love to hear from

you.

4. 2–3 minute video intro (Loom or Drive link)

5. Please share your video portfolio



***  Incomplete applications will not be reviewed. ***

title: Local SEO Specialist - Google Business Profile & Map Pack Expert
description:
Who we are



Alloy Growth Partners is a U.S.-based growth agency that works exclusively with HOA management companies — the firms that professionally manage residential communities and homeowner associations across the country. It's a large, competitive, and underserved industry, and we're the only agency built specifically for it.



Our team brings 35+ combined years of experience inside this industry. We know it from the inside out — and we build marketing, SEO, and growth systems that actually move the needle for our clients. Our mission: growth should be engineered, not hoped for.



The role



We're looking for a full-time Local SEO Specialist who owns Google Business Profile performance end-to-end for our clients. You live in the local search landscape — map packs, GBP signals, local citations, ranking factors — and you have the results to prove it.



This is not a generalist SEO role. We need someone who has done the hands-on work of moving clients into the local 3-pack in competitive markets and keeping them there. You'll manage multiple client accounts, track and report on ranking progress, and work closely with our content and web teams to make sure every local signal is aligned.



A key part of this role is the ability to audit existing local SEO strategies — including diagnosing over-reliance on aggressive tactics — and build clean, sustainable ranking foundations that hold up long-term. If you've managed ranking transitions and know how to protect a listing while shifting strategies, that experience matters here.



What you'll own

— Manage, optimize, and monitor Google Business Profile listings for multiple HOA management clients across U.S. markets

— Build and execute local SEO strategies designed to move clients into and up within the local map pack

— Audit existing local SEO setups — assess signal quality, identify risk, and build transition plans where needed

— Audit and maintain local citation profiles — NAP consistency, duplicate listings, directory accuracy

— Develop and manage review generation systems; monitor and respond to reviews strategically

— Identify and resolve GBP issues, suspensions, policy violations, and ranking drops — fast

— Track ranking performance using local SEO toolsets; benchmark against competitors and flag opportunities

— Coordinate with content and web teams to align on-page local signals with GBP strategy

— Deliver clear, consistent ranking reports that translate data into action items — not just charts

— Stay current on Google's local algorithm updates, GBP policy changes, and industry developments

— Manage your workload and deadlines in 



What we're looking for



Non-negotiables

— 3+ years of hands-on local SEO experience with a provable track record of map pack results

— Deep, current expertise in Google Business Profile optimization — categories, attributes, posts, Q&A, photos, products, and beyond

— Real examples of moving clients into the local 3-pack in competitive markets — you must be able to show your work

— Experience managing GBP for multiple clients or locations simultaneously

— Proficiency with local SEO tools: BrightLocal, Whitespark, Semrush Local, or equivalent

— Strong understanding of local ranking factors — proximity, relevance, prominence, and behavioral signals

— Ability to audit existing strategies, identify signal risk, and manage clean ranking transitions

— Ability to diagnose ranking problems systematically and build clear remediation plans

— Fluent, professional English — written and verbal

— Highly organized, detail-oriented, and accountable across multiple client accounts



Strong pluses

— Experience in HOA management, property management, or real estate

— 
 or comparable project management platform experience

— Familiarity with local link building, geo-targeted landing pages, and local schema markup

— Experience troubleshooting GBP suspensions and reinstatement

— Knowledge of review platform integrations and automated review generation workflows



What working at Alloy looks like



We operate with clear systems, defined processes, and high accountability. Everyone on the team knows what they own, how success is measured, and how their work connects to client results. We move fast, communicate clearly, and don't tolerate ambiguity sitting unaddressed.



We're a remote team, which means you'll need to be self-directed and proactive — not waiting to be told what to do next. If you thrive with ownership and hate being micromanaged, you'll fit.



How to Apply

Please submit all of the following:



— Updated Resume (PDF format)



— 2–3 minute Introduction Video (Loom, Google Drive, or similar link)

Briefly introduce yourself

Share your experience with Local SEO and Google Business Profile

Highlight specific results (e.g., moving clients into the local 3-pack)



— Portfolio or Case Studies (if available)

Examples of GBP listings you’ve managed

Before-and-after results, rankings, or performance metrics



— Answers to the following questions:

Describe a time you moved a client into the Google local 3-pack. What was your strategy?

How do you audit a Google Business Profile and identify ranking issues?

What local SEO tools have you used, and how do you use them in your workflow?

title: Design Operations Associate
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and highly organized Design Operations Associate to support our architectural and design team. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys working behind the scenes, and can keep projects, people, and systems running smoothly.



You will play a key role in ensuring the design department operates efficiently by handling administrative tasks, coordinating workflows, managing resources, and supporting the team in meeting deadlines and delivering high-quality work.



What You’ll Be Doing

- Provide efficient virtual office management support, including accurate processing of invoices, maintaining digital records, and assisting with online procurement tasks.

- Assist and coordinate with designers to ensure project tracking data is accurate and up to date.

- Help manage team accounts, assist with logins, and provide guidance on using tools such as Docusign, Dialpad, Lark Payment Requests, and others.

- Support designers with operational tasks such as sending documents via Docusign, submitting payment requests, scheduling meetings, coordinating projects, and working with other departments.

- Coordinate with consultants, request proposals, assist in scheduling site visits, track progress, and ensure timely submission of payments.

- Support the recruiting process for the design department by collating applicants from different platforms and scheduling interviews for shortlisted candidates.

- Ensure proper folder management and that all necessary documents are correctly stored in project folders.

- Assist in client contract processing by coordinating with sales, engineering, and finance teams, helping with contract drafting, payment reminders, and maintaining meeting records.

- Understand construction project processes, including phases, timelines, and deliverables, to help ensure smooth contract and project flow.

- Perform other tasks as needed to support the operations of the design team.



What We’re Looking For

- Bachelor's degree in Architecture, Engineering, or a related field.

- Proven experience in a project coordination, operations, or administrative role, preferably in a design-related environment.

- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).

- Basic knowledge of Revit, AutoCAD, and Bluebeam.

- Strong communication and presentation skills.

- Strong organizational skills with high attention to detail and accuracy.

- Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.

- Knowledge of industry-specific standards, if applicable.



Why This Role is a Great Fit

- Long-term, stable remote role with a growing design team

- Work closely with architects, designers, and project managers

- Opportunity to grow within operations and project coordination

- Exposure to real-world architectural and construction workflows



How to Apply

Please submit your application along with your resume and a brief note/portfolio about your experience in operations, or any relevant experience.

title: Finance Manager
description:
We Don't Need a Bookkeeper. We Need a Finance Manager Who Gives Us the Numbers to Make Smarter Decisions.

Finance Manager



Most finance people track what already happened. You'll give us the clarity to know where we're going—and how to get there profitably.



About Rescale Media:

We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives.

We've scaled from just drop shipping to a 40+ person team doing $1M+/month. Our goal? Multi 9-figures. And we're just getting started.

Learn more at 
dia



Our Core Values:

GENIUSES & OWNERS We partner with builders who think like founders. Not employees waiting to be told what to do—owners who see a problem and fix it.

ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.

TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.



The Role:

At $1M+/month across multiple brands, financial clarity isn't optional—it's how we make every decision.

We need someone who can manage the financial operations of a fast-moving e-commerce company. That means tracking revenue, expenses, and profitability across brands. Managing cash flow. Coordinating contractor payments for a 40+ person team. Building reports that help leadership make smarter decisions.



This isn't just data entry and bookkeeping. You'll be the person who tells us exactly where the money is going, where we're profitable, where we're bleeding, and what we need to fix. When you do your job well, we make better decisions—and better decisions compound into millions.



What You'll Do:

Manage day-to-day financial operations across multiple brands

Track revenue, expenses, and profitability with clear reporting

Manage cash flow—know where every dollar is and where it's going

Coordinate contractor payments and payroll for 40+ tea
mbers

Build and maintain financial dashboards and reports for leadership

Prepare monthly financial summaries and P&L statements

Manage budgets and flag overspending before it becomes a problem

Work with external accountants and bookkeepers as needed

Identify opportunities to reduce costs and improve margins

Support financial planning and forecasting



What We're Looking For:

2+ years of experience in finance, accounting, or financial management

Strong understanding of P&L statements, cash flow, and financial reporting

Experience managing payments and payroll (especially for contractors/remote teams)

Proficient with financial tools and spreadsheets (Excel/Google Sheets, QuickBooks, Xero, etc.)

Highly organized—you track everything and nothing slips

Detail-oriented—you catch errors before they become problems

Clear communicator—you can explain financial data to non-finance people

Proactive—you flag issues and opportunities without being asked



Bonus Points:

Experience in e-commerce or DTC companies

Familiarity with Shopify financials and payment processors (Stripe, PayPal, etc.)

Background in multi-brand or holding company structures

Experience with financial forecasting and budgeting

You've worked in a fast-paced startup or scaling company



This Role Is NOT For...

We're being direct because we respect your time:

People who only do data entry without understanding the bigger picture

Those who wait to be asked for reports instead of proactively sharing insights

Anyone who struggles with accuracy or makes careless errors with numbers

People who can't explain financial concepts simply to non-finance tea
mbers

Those who've only worked in slow-moving corporate environments

People who see finance as just "tracking the past" instead of informing the future



Compensation & Benefits:

Monthly Salary: $1,500/month



Plus:

Monthly performance bonuses tied to company Operating Profit (5-25% of salary)

Quarterly profit pool distribution (2-7.5% of company profits shared among team)

Remote-first environment

Work with a team of A-players who actually give a damn

Direct founder access—no layers of bureaucracy

Real ownership of your domain—we don't micromanage



Our Hiring Process:

We don't waste time—yours or ours. Here's how it works:

Step 1: Application (You're here!)

Step 2: Initial Interview with Founder

Step 3: Internal Assessment by Rescale

Step 4: WhatsApp update for next steps

Step 5: Signing of Independent Contractor and NDA

Step 6: Onboarding and Training

Total timeline: 1 week from application to offer.



Ready to Apply?



Complete the application form above. Be thorough—this is your first impression.



P.S. Great finance management is invisible—the numbers are always right, the reports are always ready, and leadership always knows where they stand. If you're the type who makes financial chaos disappear, we want to talk.

P.P.S. If you're reading this far, you're already more thorough than 90% of applicants. That's exactly who we want.

? ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.

???? TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.



The Role

Every visitor we acquire costs money. Your job is to extract maximum value from each one—across the entire funnel.

This isn't a "tweak the headline and call it a day" role. You'll own optimization from the 
nt someone clicks an ad to the 
nt they complete their final upsell purchase.

The funnel you'll own:

Advertorials - Pre-sell pages that warm up traffic

Sales Pages - Core offer pages that convert browsers to buyers

Upsell Sequences - Post-purchase flows that maximize AOV

We're doing millions in revenue. A 10% lift at any stage compounds across the entire funnel. If you can systematically improve each step, you'll be one of the most valuable people in the company.



What You'll Do

Own full-funnel conversion optimization across all brand sites

Build complete upsell sequences from scratch - not just optimize existing ones

Analyze user behavior data to identify friction and drop-off points

Develop and prioritize testing roadmap based on revenue impact

Design and run A/B tests (advertorials, sales pages, checkout, upsells)

Analyze test results and document learnings

Collaborate with designers and developers on implementation

Monitor funnel performance and identify issues before they impact revenue

Build dashboards to track conversion metrics at every stage



What We're Looking For

2+ years of CRO experience in e-commerce

Proven experience building upsell sequences from scratch (this is non-negotiable)

Experience optimizing advertorials and long-form sales pages

Strong analytics skills (Microsoft Clarity, heatmaps, session recordings)

Understanding of statistical significance and experiment design

Experience with Shopify or similar e-commerce platforms

Ability to write clear test hypotheses and document results

Bonus Points:

Experience with high-traffic sites (100K+ monthly visitors)

Background in direct response copywriting

Experience with post-purchase upsell tools (CartHook, ReConvert, etc.)

You've delivered measurable AOV lifts through upsell optimization (and can prove it)



This Role Is NOT For...

We're being direct because we respect your time:

People who have only optimized landing pages, not full funnels

Those who tweak existing upsell pages but have never built one from scratch

Anyone who can't explain the math behind statistical significance

Those who can't prioritize high-impact over easy wins

title: Creative Strategist
description:
We Don't Need a Creative Strategist. We Need a Revenue-Generating Idea Machine.

Most creative strategists make pretty ads. You'll engineer the concepts that scale to millions in profitable revenue.



About Rescale Media

We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives.

We've scaled from just drop shipping to a 30+ person team doing $1M+/month. Our goal? Multi 9-figures. And we're just getting started. Learn more at 
dia



Our Core Values

- GENIUSES & OWNERS We partner with builders who think like founders. Not employees waiting to be told what to do—owners who see a problem and fix it.

- ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.

- TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.



The Role

This is the role that moves the needle more than any other. Our Creative Strategists don't just come up with ideas—they architect the concepts, angles, and hooks that turn cold audiences into raving customers.

You'll work at the intersection of psychology, data, and creativity. Every concept you develop will be tested, measured, and either scaled or killed based on real performance data.

Our brands serve faith-led families and parents who want magic in their children's lives. You'll create advertising that resonates deeply with these audiences—not cheap manipulation, but genuine connection.



We require at least 3-5 concepts per week and are open to working with freelancers.



What You'll Do:

- Develop creative concepts, angles, and hooks that drive profitable customer acquisition

- Write ad scripts, headlines, and copy that convert

- Analyze performance data to understand what's working and why

- Brief video editors and designers on creative execution

- Manage the creative testing pipeline—always have winners in the queue

- Stay on top of creative trends, competitor ads, and market dynamics

- Collaborate with Media Buyers to understand what's scaling and what's not

- Build and maintain our creative knowledge base (what works for our brands)



What We're Looking For

- 3+ years of creative strategy experience in DTC/e-commerce

- Portfolio of ads you've created that scaled to significant spend

- Deep understanding of direct response principles

- Strong copywriting skills—you can write a hook that stops the scroll

- Data literacy—you can read a dashboard and extract insights

- Experience briefing creative teams (video editors, designers)

- Understanding of Meta's ad ecosystem and creative best practices



Bonus Points:

- Experience with faith-based or family-oriented brands

- Background in UGC strategy and influencer creative

- You've built creative processes from scratch

- Performance marketing or media buying experience

- Strong understanding of consumer psychology



This Role Is NOT For...

We're being direct because we respect your time:

- Creatives who care more about awards than results

- Those who can't take feedback on their "babies"

- Anyone who thinks strategy and execution are separate jobs

- People who aren't comfortable with rapid iteration and testing



Compensation & Benefits

- Monthly Salary: $2,000 - $3,000/month

Plus:

- Monthly performance bonuses tied to company Operating Profit (5-25% of salary)

- Quarterly profit pool distribution (2-7.5% of company profits shared among team)

- Ad Bounty Program: Earn 2-7% commission on ad spend for high-performing creatives (ROAS 2.1+)

- Remote-first environment (European timezone preferred)

- Work with a team of A-players who actually give a damn

- Direct founder access—no layers of bureaucracy

- Real ownership of your domain—we don't micromanage



Our Hiring Process

We don't waste time—yours or ours. Here's how it works:

Step 1: Application (You're here!)

Step 2: Initial Interview with Founder

Step 3: Internal Assessment by Rescale

Step 4: WhatsApp update for next steps

Step 5: Signing of Independent Contractor and NDA

Step 6: Onboarding and Training



Total timeline: 1 week from application to offer.



Ready to Apply?

[  
  ]

Complete the application form above. Be thorough—this is your first impression.



P.S. Our best-performing ad ever was a simple concept that took 20 minutes to write. It's generated over $1M in revenue. If you understand why simplicity often wins, we should talk.

P.P.S. If you're reading this far, you're already more thorough than 90% of applicants. That's exactly who we want.

title: Fire Alarm / Low Voltage Systems Designer ($1280/ month)
description:
Location: Remote



Compensation: $8–$11/hour (based on experience)



About the Role:

We are seeking a skilled Fire Alarm / Security Systems Designer to join our team on a freelance, part-time, or full-time basis. The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise in fire alarm systems, security systems, CCTV, and access control. Projects will include system design, drafting, and documentation for submittals and installations.



Responsibilities:

- Design fire alarm, security, CCTV, and access control systems in compliance with NFPA, IBC, IFC, and local codes.

- Produce professional drawings and submittal packages using AutoDesk AutoCAD.

- Coordinate system layouts, device locations, riser diagrams, and wiring details.

- Collaborate with project managers, contractors, and AHJs as needed.

- Revise and update designs based on feedback or code requirements.



Qualifications:

- Proven experience designing fire alarm and/or low voltage security systems.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD (required).

- Familiarity with NFPA 72 and other relevant fire/life safety codes.

- Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines.

- Good communication skills and ability to work independently.



Preferred (not required):

- NICET certification in Fire Alarm Systems or equivalent experience.

- Experience preparing full submittal packages for AHJ approval.

- Knowledge of manufacturer-specific equipment (e.g., Fire Lite, DMP, Kidde, Silent Knight, Honeywell, etc.).



To Apply:

- Resume (required).

- Sample projects or work portfolio (preferred).

- Availability (freelance/part
-time).

- Brief summary of relevant experience.

title: Experienced Fire Alarm / Security Systems Designer ($1280/ month) Remote)
description:
Experienced Fire Alarm / Security Systems Designer (Remote)



Location: Remote

Compensation: $8–$10/hour (based on experience)



We are a fire & security contracting and engineering company seeking for  a skilled Fire Alarm / Security Systems Designer to join our team on a freelance, part-time, or full-time basis. The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise in fire alarm systems, security systems, CCTV, and access control. Projects will include system design, drafting, and documentation for submittals and installations.



Key Responsibilities



- Design fire alarm, security, CCTV, and access control systems in compliance with NFPA, IBC, IFC, and local codes.

- Produce professional drawings and submittal packages using AutoDesk AutoCAD.

- Coordinate system layouts, device locations, riser diagrams, and wiring details.

- Collaborate with project managers, contractors, and AHJs as needed.

- Revise and update designs based on feedback or code requirements.



Qualifications:

- Proven experience designing fire alarm and/or low voltage security systems.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD (required).

- Familiarity with NFPA 72 and other relevant fire/life safety codes.

- Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines.

- Good communication skills and ability to work independently.



Preferred (not required):

- NICET certification in Fire Alarm Systems or equivalent experience.

- Experience preparing full submittal packages for AHJ approval.

- Knowledge of manufacturer-specific equipment (e.g., Fire Lite, DMP, Kidde, Silent Knight, Honeywell, etc.).



Position Details

-Flexible arrangement: freelance, part-time, or full-time

-Remote: work from anywhere

-Compensation: $6–$10/hour (based on experience and performance)

-Must be responsive and available to coordinate during business hours



Growth & Career Path



We’re building for the long term, and the right candidate will have the opportunity to grow with us:

-Potential to transition to a full-time or Engineering Lead role.

-Be part of a fast-growing, tech-enabled contracting & engineering company that values operational excellence and innovation.



Application Instructions (Read Carefully)



To apply, you must complete all the steps below:



1.) Write a short paragraph explaining why you are a good fit for this role.



2.) Answer this logic/comprehension question in 2–3 sentences:

          “If you are designing a fire alarm system and the AHJ requests additional devices after your initial submittal, how would you handle the change?”



3.) Use this subject line in your application
:

          “Designer Application – [Your Name]”



4.) At the very end of your application, include this exact phrase:

           “I want to synergize with you”



5.) Submit a 2-minute video explaining your experience in fire alarm/security system design (portfolio samples encouraged).          

           **Upload via Google Drive or Loom and share the link.**

title: Legal Intake VA
description:
Pay: $1,060/month during training (3 months), $1,250/month after

Hours: 40 hrs/week | 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Chicago Time (night shift for PH)

Location preference: Philippines



We're a busy divorce law firm based in Chicago helping families navigate some of the hardest 
nts of their lives. Our clients are going through separation, custody disputes, and the emotional weight that comes with all of it — and the first voice they hear when they reach out matters enormously. We take that seriously, and we need someone on our team who does too.



As our Legal Intake VA, you'll be the first point of contact for people who are often scared, overwhelmed, or unsure of what to do next. Your job is to answer calls and web inquiries quickly, warmly, and professionally — converting those 
nts of vulnerability into scheduled consultations with our attorneys. You'll work closely with our intake team to keep the calendar organized, the leads followed up on, and the experience seamless from first hello to confirmed appointment.



— Immediate lead response: When a new lead submits a form on our website, you call them back — fast. Speed-to-lead is everything in legal intake, and your quick response is often the difference between a booked consult and a lost client.



— Confident, compassionate phone presence: You'll answer inbound calls with energy and warmth, guiding potential clients through the scheduling process with empathy and professionalism — never robotic, always human.



— Lead follow-up and nurturing: Leads who haven't scheduled yet don't get forgotten. You'll manage consistent outreach through calls and messages, keeping our pipeline warm and moving.



— Appointment management: You'll assist the intake team with confirming, rescheduling, and canceling appointments — keeping attorney calendars accurate and clients properly informed.



— CRM and notes hygiene: Every call, every interaction, every outcome gets logged. You'll keep our intake records clean and up to date so nothing falls through the cracks.



— Schedule coordination support: You'll work alongside our intake team to make sure daily scheduling runs smoothly, flagging any conflicts or gaps that need attention.



- Near-perfect American English — you speak clearly and confidently with little to no accent; our clients must feel at ease immediately

- Minimum 2 years of phone-based work experience — customer service, appointment setting, or similar

- Comfortable making high volumes of outbound calls and handling inbound inquiries without hesitation

- Attention to detail: Include the phrase, "Big journeys begin with small steps" in your cover letter.

- Prior experience in a law firm, medical office, or other sensitive client-facing environment is a strong plus

- Excellent written English for follow-up messaging and CRM notes

- Hardworking, honest, and genuinely caring — you treat every caller like they matter, because they do

- Fast learner who can pick up our processes, scripts, and software quickly

- Comfortable working night shift hours (8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Chicago time)

- Quiet, professional home workspace free of background noise

- Fast, reliable internet connection

- Proficient with Google Workspace and/or Microsoft Office; CRM experience is a plus



This job is not for companies, contractors with multiple clients, or those who have never worked online. Side projects are not allowed.



Training rate is $1,060/month for the first three months, moving to $1,250/month upon successful completion. Pay is sent monthly via Wise.



- 13th month bonus (one month's pay, prorated for partial years)

- Christmas week off (paid)

- Birthday off (paid)

- 7 floating PTO days/year after 3 months

- SSS reimbursement up to $50/mo

- PhilHealth reimbursement up to $50/mo

- Private health insurance reimbursement up to $30/mo

- Discretionary equipment & education reimbursement



You supply your own modern computer and reliable high-speed internet.



Click the application link below. Submit your resume, intro video, and a brief cover letter.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Excellent written and spoken English

- Quiet workspace, fast internet

- Long-term, full-time only



Important: please include the phrase "Big journeys begin with small steps." in your cover letter — applications without this phrase will not be considered.



PAY RATE: $1,060/month during training (3 months), $1,250/month after, paid via Wise.

HOURS PER WEEK: 40 hours.



HOW TO APPLY: Click the link below to apply through our secure portal:





We'll review your application and get back to you within 48 hours.
