title: Assistant Property Manager
description:
We manage a well-maintained portfolio of single-family homes with high-quality, creditworthy tenants. We’re looking for a detail-oriented and highly organized professional who takes pride in delivering excellent service and keeping operations running smoothly.



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys the full scope of property management—from leasing and tenant communication to maintenance coordination—and is proactive, responsive, and solution-driven.



The ideal candidate has strong customer service skills and at least one year of leasing experience using AppFolio. Experience with video editing and website development or updates using WIX is a plus, especially for enhancing listings and marketing our properties.



If you’re someone who communicates clearly, follows through, and enjoys being a key part of a growing property management operation, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Permitting Coordinator (Architecture / Residential Projects)
description:
Position Summary:

We are seeking a highly organized and proactive architect to oversee the permit application process for residential projects in California. This role will involve coordinating with local building departments, ensuring compliance with regulations, and expediting permit approvals to keep projects moving efficiently. The ideal candidate will work closely with the design department to prepare required documentation, monitor project timelines, and communicate confidently with city officials, consultants, and internal teams.



Key Responsibilities:

- Manage the permit application process for residential projects, ensuring all submissions meet state and local regulatory requirements.

- Work closely with the design department to gather necessary plans, reports, and specifications for permit submissions.

- Liaise with city officials, building departments, and third-party consultants to facilitate approvals and resolve any permitting issues.

- Track and monitor permit timelines, providing regular updates to internal stakeholders to maintain efficient progress.

- Identify opportunities to expedite permit approvals by proactively addressing compliance requirements and coordinating necessary revisions.

- Ensure adherence to California building codes, zoning laws, and environmental regulations in all permit applications.

- Maintain organized documentation for permits, approvals, and project correspondence.

- Stay informed of changes in local permitting policies that may affect project timelines and requirements.



Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s degree in Architecture, Construction Management, Urban Planning.

- Licensed Architect (preferred) or relevant experience in permit coordination for residential projects.

- Strong knowledge of California building codes, zoning regulations, and permit processes.

- Experience working with city agencies, planning boards, and regulatory bodies.

- Exceptional organizational skills and the ability to track multiple projects efficiently.

- Highly confident communicator with strong negotiation and problem-solving abilities.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD, Revit, and other architectural documentation tools.

- Familiarity with permit expediting strategies and local jurisdiction requirements.



Send your CV and Portfolio, make sure it is accessible. Goodluck!

title: Cold Caller / Outbound Sales Development Representative
description:
About TaskOn eCommerce

TaskOn eCommerce is a Klaviyo Master Platinum Partner and retention marketing agency. We help 7–8 figure DTC brands on Shopify maximize revenue through
, SMS, loyalty, and owned-channel marketing. We also manage Amazon Vendor Central (1P) for select clients. Our team works with brands in health & wellness, pet supplies, beauty, and sports/outdoor verticals.



About the Role

We’re looking for a motivated and professional Cold Caller to join our team. Your primary responsibility is to reach out to DTC brand founders, eCommerce directors, and Amazon decision-makers on behalf of TaskOn eCommerce and book qualified discovery calls with our leadership team.



This is a high-activity outbound role. You will be provided with background on TaskOn eCommerce’s services, value proposition, and target client profile to guide your conversations. Scripts and call frameworks will be developed collaboratively over time. Your job is to make contact, build rapport quickly, communicate what we do clearly, and secure a booked discovery call.



Please note: this role requires working Pacific Standard Time (PST) hours. Candidates who are unable to work PST hours will not be considered.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Making outbound calls to DTC brand founders and key decision-makers

• Following provided call frameworks while adapting naturally to the conversation

• Qualifying prospects based on criteria provided by leadership

• Booking discovery calls directly onto the calendar of our President & Founder

• Logging all activity, call outcomes, and notes in our CRM

• Following up with prospects via phone and
 as needed

• Providing daily or weekly activity summaries to leadership



What We’re Looking For

• 3+ years with demonstrated results in cold calling and booking meetings - this is a firm requirement

• Fluent English speaker with clear, professional phone communication skills

• Proven track record of booking qualified meetings with business owners and decision-makers

• Comfortable speaking with founders and senior-level contacts

• Highly organized, self-motivated, and accountable

• Coachable - you follow provided frameworks while bringing your own energy and initiative

• Reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional calling environment

• Familiarity with CRM tools and call tracking is a plus

• Knowledge of eCommerce, Shopify, and digital marketing is a strong plus 



What to Expect in the First 90 Days

This role comes with clearly defined 30, 60, and 90-day goals that will be shared upon hire. These are firm targets, and we believe the right person will see them as a clear roadmap for success rather than a hurdle.



What Success Looks Like

• A consistent volume of outbound calls made daily

• Qualified discovery calls booked onto leadership’s calendar

• Clean, accurate logging of all call activity

• Achieving the target goals set



Compensation

This role offers a base salary plus a two-tier commission structure. You will earn a commission for each completed discovery call that meets our qualification criteria, and a larger commission bonus when a prospect converts into a paying client. This structure rewards both consistent activity and results.



To Apply

Please submit your resume along with a short voice recording (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you’d be a great fit for this role. Applications without a voice recording will not be considered.

title: Finance Director
description:
Finance Director — Strategic Finance Leader for Multiple 8–9 Figure Businesses | $3,000/month USD

We Don't Need a Reporter. We Need a Finance Leader Who Helps Us D
 Where to Bet Next.

Most finance people tell you what already happened. You'll tell us what to do next — and own the financial strategy across multiple brands and a SaaS company.

About Rescale Media

We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives — products rooted in faith, tradition, and timeless values that resist fleeting modern trends.

Our portfolio includes health and beauty brands, plus 
 — our financial intelligence and fractional CFO platform supporting e-commerce founders worldwide.

We've scaled past $2M+/month with a global team, and we're targeting multi 9-figures. The next 12–24 months will be defined by capital allocation, margin expansion, and disciplined scaling decisions — and that's exactly where this role lives.

???? Learn more at 
dia

Our Core Values

???? GENIUSES & OWNERS We partner with builders who think like founders. Not finance people waiting for instructions — operators who see a margin problem, build the model, and walk into the CEO's office with a recommendation.

? ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can pressure-test a hypothesis, refine the forecast, and turn data into a better decision. Build it. Stress-test it. Improve it. Repeat.

???? TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. If a brand is unprofitable, you say it. If a campaign is bleeding cash, you flag it. If leadership is wrong, you push back with data. No politics. No spin. Just truth.

The Role

This is not a traditional finance reporting role. This is a builder role for someone who wants to operate as a Fractional CFO across an entire portfolio of businesses.

You will:

Sit at the right hand of the CEO as a strategic thought partner

Own financial strategy across multiple brands and 

Drive decisions on capital allocation, scaling, pricing, and profitability

Manage and develop the Finance Manager (your direct report)

Build the financial backbone that allows us to scale to multi 9-figures without losing control of the numbers

If you've been the smartest finance person in every room and you're tired of presenting reports to leaders who don't act on them — this is your seat.

What You'll Own

Strategic Finance & FP&A

Own financial strategy across all business units (multiple DTC brands + SaaS)

Build and maintain advanced financial models — actuals reporting, forecasting, scenario planning, cash flow projections

Translate complex financial data into clear, actionable decisions for the CEO and leadership

Prepare investor-ready materials and supporting analysis as needed

Unit Economics & Performance

Analyze CAC, LTV, contribution margin, operating margin, and blended ROAS across brands

Deliver weekly and monthly performance insights that drive real decisions

Identify margin leaks, inefficient spend, and cost optimization opportunities before they become problems

Capital & Cash Flow

Manage cash flow planning across multiple entities

Drive capital allocation decisions — where to invest, where to cut, where to scale

Implement financial reporting structures that give leadership a real-time view of the business

Leadership & Operations

Manage and develop the Finance Manager — ensuring strong execution of accounting operations and reporting

Support pricing, margin, and operational efficiency initiatives across departments

Partner with department leads to align financial reality with operational decisions

What We're Looking For

5–10+ years in finance with proven leadership experience

Strong e-commerce / DTC experience — non-negotiable. You understand how this industry actually makes money.

Deep expertise in financial modeling and forecasting — not just maintaining models, but building them from scratch

Strong grasp of unit economics and the growth levers that move the business

Direct experience working with founders or operators — not just middle management

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets skills — modeling, scenario analysis, automation

Operator mindset — you don't just analyze, you make calls

Sharp communicator — you can take a 50-line P&L and make a CEO understand the one thing that matters in 30 seconds

Bonus Points

Experience operating in a Fractional CFO capacity

Experience managing multiple entities, brands, or business units

SaaS finance experience alongside DTC

Experience managing and developing junior finance talent

Comfort with 
 or similar e-commerce financial intelligence tools

Resonance with our mission to build faith-led, traditional, value-driven brands

This Role Is NOT For:

People who want to be told what to do

Reporters who track the past but can't predict the future

Finance people who hide behind spreadsheets instead of making recommendations

Anyone who needs ego massaging or office politics

People uncomfortable with founder-level directness or pushing back when leadership is wrong

Anyone looking for a clock-in, clock-out role

People who don't resonate with our mission of building traditional, faith-led, value-driven brands

If you read those bullets and felt defensive, this isn't the role for you. Move on. If you read them and thought "finally, a place that gets it" — keep reading.

Compensation & Benefits

Base Salary: $3,000 USD/month (full-time)

Performance-Based Bonuses:

Individual Monthly Bonus: Up to 25% of monthly salary based on company Operating Profit %, with double bonus % as a Department Lead (up to 50%)

Quarterly Profit Pool: Equal share of the Employee Profit Pool (2.0%–7.5% of operating profit, scaling with monthly gross sales)

Long-term profit and/or equity incentives for the right person

Other Benefits:

Fully remote

European team orientation

Direct line to the CEO — your work moves the business

Front-row seat to scaling multiple 8–9 figure brands plus a SaaS platform

Our Hiring Process

We don't do gut-feel hiring. We do standards and data.

Application — Submit through the form below

Screening / Task Test — A short, role-specific exercise to see how you actually think

Founder Interview — Direct conversation with the CEO

Paid Trial Project — Real work, real compensation, real signal

Ready to Apply?

[ 
 ]

Complete the application form above. Be thorough — this is your first impression.

Please attach your resume.

P.S. The best finance leaders aren't reporting on the past — they're shaping the future. If you've ever sat in a leadership meeting and thought "I could be running the financial strategy here" — this is your seat.

P.P.S. We're not the biggest, but we're building something that matters. If a mission to build faith-led, traditional, value-driven brands resonates with you — you've found your team.

title: Project Coordinator
description:
Role: Project Coordinator (Team Coordinator)

Hours: Full Time, Remote, EST Hours

Us:  A growing restoration company



The Project Coordinator supports the successful execution of restoration projects by managing scheduling, customer communication, vendor coordination and administrative processes.  This role ensures that estimators and project managers are supported with accurate information, organized workflows, and timely communication so that projects run smoothly from start to finish.

Customer Service & Communication

Billing & Documentation Support

Administrative Support

Process Improvement

QUALIFICATIONS

? Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment

Preferred:

Bonus if you have worked with EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating Systems) companies previously.

title: Quantity Takeoff Estimator
description:
US. Central Time 8-5 M-F Full time position



Quantity Takeoff / Junior Estimator

(Masonry, Concrete & Hardscape)



Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented Quantity Takeoff Specialist to support our estimating team

in a growing masonry, fencing, concrete, and hardscape company. This role is designed as a

development position, with the goal of training the right candidate into a full-time

Commercial Estimator.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who wants to build a long-term career in

construction estimating and project management within the U.S. commercial construction

industry.



Key Responsibilities

• Perform quantity takeoffs from plans and specifications for masonry, fencing,

hardscape, and concrete work

• Interpret architectural, civil, and structural drawings

• Apply U.S. construction standards, building codes, and specifications

• Measure materials using U.S. units (SF, LF, CY, tons, etc.)

• Break down concrete scopes including yardage, rebar quantities, and formwork

• Organize quantities into estimating spreadsheets and templates

• Assist with bid preparation, scope reviews, and proposals

• Maintain organized documentation of all takeoffs



Growth Path

This role is intended to develop into a Commercial Estimator position, including building

full project estimates, pricing labor and materials, understanding code requirements,

managing bids, and interacting with contractors and clients.



Qualifications

• Required:

• Strong attention to detail

• Ability to read construction drawings

• Familiarity with U.S. construction units

• Basic understanding of U.S. building codes

• Proficiency in Excel



• Strong organizational skills

• Preferred:

• Experience with takeoff software (PlanSwift, Bluebeam, OST)

• Experience with concrete and rebar takeoffs

• Background in masonry or site work

• Estimating or project coordination experience



What We’re Looking For

• Desire to grow into a commercial estimator role

• Comfort working with detailed plans and specs

• Strong problem-solving mindset

• Reliable and deadline-driven

• Interest in a long-term construction career



Why This Role is Different

This is not just a takeoff position. We are hiring and training someone to become a key

estimator capable of understanding full project scope, including code requirements and

constructability.

title: Outreach Virtual Assistant — DrRomulusMBA (LinkedIn DMs + GHL Pipeline)
description:
About us




anna Romulus helps service-based founders — consultants, coaches, and agency owners — install the systems behind predictable revenue. Our methodology is the DDS Framework: Diagnose, Design, Scale. Structure before scale.



The role



You will be the first touchpoint for our Revenue Architecture Session pipeline. Your job is to put qualified founders on Dr. Romulus's calendar — using personalized outbound, clean follow-up, and disciplined CRM hygiene. This is a conversion role, not a content role. We measure in booked calls, not messages sent.



What you will own



1. Outbound DM and
 — 20 to 40 personalized first-touches per day across LinkedIn (primary) and
, plus a structured 2-step follow-up sequence. Scripts will be approved and provided.



2. GHL pipeline management — every conversation logged in GoHighLevel, opportunity stages updated daily, booking link delivered at the right 
nt, no leads lost.



3. Lead research and list building — daily ICP prospect list of 20 to 50 profiles sourced from LinkedIn and Apollo, personalization angles documented before any outreach goes out.



Tools you will work in



GoHighLevel (GHL), LinkedIn (Sales Navigator helpful, not required), Apollo, Notion, Loom,
 or Slack, ChatGPT or Claude.



What we are looking for



— 2 or more years of appointment-setting or outbound work for service-based businesses, ideally coaches or consultants

— Native-level written English; your DM voice should be indistinguishable from a US-based founder

— LinkedIn outbound experience with a result you can name in numbers (X messages sent ? Y meetings booked)

— Comfort inside GHL or a comparable CRM with daily pipeline hygiene

— AI tool fluency (ChatGPT, Claude, Apollo) used in your real workflow today

— Direct-to-founder experience preferred — we hire the operator, not the agency



Compensation

$500 to $700 per month USD, paid monthly via Payoneer or PayPal (your choice). Final rate set in interview based on experience. 20 to 30 hours per week.



How to apply



Reply with three things:



1. One paragraph — why this role, specifically.

2. Your best appointment-setting result. Name the business, the channel, and the number.

Dr. Romulus

ANAMECHI Marketing

title: Video Editor - Real Estate
description:
To apply, complete the Google Form linked in this posting.





Applications submitted directly through OnlineJobs.ph will not be reviewed.



ABOUT ALLISON MEDIA CO.



Allison Media Co. is a premium real estate media company serving high-performing agents and developers across Ontario — from Niagara to Toronto. We produce photo, video, aerial, and marketing content that helps properties sell and brands stand out.



We're growing fast. Volume is up. Our standard is high. And we need editors who can keep up.



WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS



This isn't a filler role. We have real estate shoots happening daily and a growing backlog of video content that needs to be edited to a premium standard — fast.



The edit is where the brand lives. Every video we deliver goes to an agent's listing, a developer's launch, or a brand's social presence. Your work directly impacts how our clients are perceived and how our business grows.



We need someone who can jump in, learn the standard quickly, and start cutting clips that are clean, consistent, and on-brand — immediately.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



This is a high-volume, high-standard editing role. You'll be responsible for:



Editing 3–5 real estate video packages per day

Working primarily in Premiere Pro and DaVinci Resolve

Following AMC's editing style, pacing, and color guidelines

Catching your own errors before files are submitted for review

Organizing and managing your own project files cleanly

Communicating clearly with the lead editor and owner on status and flags

Improving your output quality continuously based on feedback



HOW YOU WIN HERE



You'll know you're succeeding when:



Your edits are consistently clean and require minimal revisions

You're hitting turnaround targets without sacrificing quality

The team trusts your files without needing to double-check everything

Your output is improving week over week

Clients are proud to post what we deliver



REQUIREMENTS



Non-negotiable:



Real estate video editing experience — you must have a portfolio that proves it

Proficiency in Premiere Pro and/or DaVinci Resolve

Strong understanding of pacing, color, and storytelling for property video

Ability to work full-time immediately upon hiring

Reliable internet, computer, and professional editing setup

Strong written English for team communication

High attention to detail — you catch mistakes before anyone else does

Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously without losing quality



WHO THIS IS FOR



You've edited real estate content before and you're good at it

You work fast without getting sloppy

You take feedback seriously and apply it on the next edit, not the one after

You want consistent full-time volume, not sporadic freelance work

You're self-directed — you don't need to be told what to do next

You care about the quality of your work, not just getting files out the door



WHO THIS IS NOT FOR



Editors without real estate experience

Anyone who avoids feedback or gets defensive about revisions

People who ship 'good enough' work

Freelancers looking for passive, low-commitment work

Anyone who needs constant direction to stay on task



COMPENSATION



Starting Rate:

$6–$10 CAD per hour, based on experience and portfolio quality



Growth:

Performance-based — not time-based. Strong editors grow fast here.



OUR CORE VALUES



These are operating requirements — not wall art.



Extreme Ownership

We take full responsibility for outcomes. We fix problems before they reach the client, follow through, and don't make excuses.



Flexible Excellence

We adapt to real-world challenges without dropping our standard. Speed matters, but polish is non-negotiable.



Professional Confidence

We show up with calm, capable leadership. We guide the client, lead on-site, and carry ourselves like a part of their brand.



Sincere Accountability

We give and receive feedback with clarity, humility, and care. We call each other higher, not out.



HOW TO APPLY



Submit your application through the Google Form linked in this posting. Direct applications through OnlineJobs.ph will not be reviewed.



Your application must include:



Your best real estate video or demo reel (link required)

A 1–2 minute video introduction: who you are, what you're proud of, and why you're the right fit for AMC



We are not looking for the most experienced editor.

We are looking for the right one.



To apply, complete the Google Form linked in this posting.

title: Full-Stack AI Engineer Experienced to Develop AI based softwares and database
description:
We are looking for an exceptional AI Engineer to help us build a powerfull AI platform from the ground up.



Our vision is to create one central AI platform where our team, users, and customers can solve problems, automate work, generate content, analyze data, and access advanced AI capabilities without depending on multiple outside tools. We want to build a serious product with long-term potential.



We are looking for someone who is not just skilled, but deeply excited by the challenge.





This role is for someone who genuinely loves building, solving hard problems, and turning big ideas into real products. We can provide resources, tools, support, and room to build. What we need from you is strong engineering ability, deep curiosity, ownership, and the hunger to create something meaningful.

What We Want to Build



We want to build an AI platform that can eventually include



features like:

conversational AI

AI agents and workflows

document analysis and knowledge retrieval

content generation

business automation

internal team productivity tools

integrations with different tools, systems, and databases

private/company-specific AI usage in one ecosystem

scalable infrastructure for future growth

The long-term goal is to create a platform that reduces the need to jump between multiple AI tools and gives users one strong system to work from.



What You’ll Do

You will help lead the technical build of the platform, including:

designing the architecture for the AI platform

building AI-powered product features end-to-end

working on backend systems, APIs, databases, and model integration

helping define the product roadmap from an engineering perspective

building clean, scalable, maintainable systems

improving speed, accuracy, performance, and usability

creating secure and reliable workflows for users and internal teams

collaborating closely with leadership to turn vision into execution



What We’re Looking For

We want someone with strong experience in areas such as:

AI/ML engineering

LLM application development

full-stack product engineering

backend architecture and APIs

vector databases / retrieval systems / RAG

agent workflows and AI orchestration

model deployment and inference optimization

cloud infrastructure and scaling

product thinking and execution

You should be comfortable working with things like:

Python

FastAPI or similar backend frameworks

React / Next.js or similar frontend tools

Docker

cloud platforms

databases

AI infrastructure

open-source model ecosystems

performance optimization

security and reliability best practices

Ideal Candidate

You may be a great fit if you:

have built real AI products, not just demos

can think like both an engineer and a builder

enjoy solving difficult technical problems

can work independently and move fast

understand how to turn ambitious ideas into practical systems

are excited by building something big from the ground up

care deeply about quality, speed, and ownership

Important

This role is for builders.

If you only want routine tasks, this is definintely not the right fit.

If you get excited by hard challenges, big vision, product ownership, and the chance to help build something serious, we want to hear from you.

We are ready to support the right person with resources, tools, and long-term opportunity. We want someone who sees this as more than just another freelance gig.



To Apply

Please send:

a short introduction about yourself

your relevant AI/product engineering experience

links to platforms, products, or systems you’ve built

the AI stack/tools/frameworks you know best

an example of a difficult problem you solved

how you would approach building an all-in-one AI platform from scratch

why this opportunity interests you



Bonus Points

Extra strong if you have experience with:

private/self-hosted AI systems

enterprise AI tools

multi-agent systems

model fine-tuning

inference optimization

AI product architecture

SaaS product development

startup or founding engineer experience

title: Virtual Assistant (Sales-Focused)
description:
A growing U.S.-based cleaning company focused on delivering excellent service and consistent growth. We are expanding and need strong communicators who can help us convert leads into booked jobs.



We are not just looking for an assistant—we are looking for someone who is *hungry, driven, and motivated to make money through performance and commissions.*



---

This role is a mix of *Virtual Assistant + Sales Representative.*



You will be responsible for handling calls, following up with leads, and *turning inquiries into booked appointments.* If you are confident on the phone and enjoy closing deals, this role is for you.



---



*Work Schedule (CST – U.S. Time Zone)*



* Monday to Friday

* Training: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM CST

* After training: 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM CST

* 5 hours per day (part-time to start)

* Must be flexible—some days may require extended hours

* Opportunity to move into full-time based on performance



---



*Compensation*



* $5/hour during 2–3 week probation

* $6/hour after successful probation

* *Commission opportunities available based on bookings and performance*

* Increased pay and hours for high performers

---

*Key Responsibilities*



* Handle inbound and outbound calls

* Follow up with leads quickly and consistently

* Convert inquiries into booked cleaning appointments

* Schedule jobs and update customer records

* Deliver professional, confident, and friendly customer service

* Support admin tasks as needed



---



*What We’re REALLY Looking For*



* Strong *sales mindset* (this is NOT just admin work)

* Confident speaking on the phone and handling objections

* Experience with *cold calling, lead follow-up, or closing sales*

* Fast response time and strong follow-through

* Self-motivated and performance-driven

* Reliable, organized, and able to work independently



---

*BONUS (Not Required but Preferred)*



* Experience working with a U.S. cleaning company

* Experience in appointment setting or call center sales

---

*Important – Read Before Applying*



We ONLY consider applicants who follow instructions.



To apply, you MUST submit:



1. A *1–2 minute VIDEO recording* (not audio) answering:



   * Your sales experience

   * How you handle objections or difficult customers

   * Why you are a good fit for a sales-focused role



2. A link to your resume (*make sure access is public*)



*If you do NOT submit a video, your application will be automatically rejected.*



Start your application with: *“I’m ready to close deals”*



---



We are looking for someone who wants to grow, earn more, and be part of a fast-moving team—not someone just looking for a basic VA job.

description:

 address

title: Lead Video Editor – (TikTok & Instagram)
description:
We are seeking a Lead Video Editor to serve as the creative backbone of our agency. You will be responsible for executing our proprietary playbook and currently trending video formats, designed specifically to convert on TikTok Shop and Instagram (eventually Instagram Shop). You are not just making social media posts; you are creating high-end digital commercials that drive affiliate revenue. Part with growth potential to full time. 



[RESPONSIBILITIES]



The "Garde" Playbook: Edit high-converting, vertical videos (9:16) designed specifically for the TikTok Shop and Instagram algorithms.



Advanced Typography: Seamlessly integrate sophisticated, magazine-quality text overlays to elevate standard affiliate content.



Color Grading & Pacing: Apply professional color grading to raw product and lifestyle footage to achieve a "Vogue meets journal" aesthetic, while maintaining the fast hook-pacing required for social commerce.



Creative Direction: Help set the visual standard for the agency. When we scale and hire junior editors, your work will be the gold standard they follow.



[RESPONSIBILITIES]



Social Commerce Architecture: Edit high-converting, vertical videos (9:16) for the TikTok Shop Affiliate ecosystem. You must know how to balance a "Native" algorithmic feel with a high-conversion, "shopper" hook.



The "Garde" Playbook: Apply professional color grading and cinematic framing to raw product/lifestyle footage to achieve a luxurious, "Vogue meets journal" aesthetic.



Advanced Typography: Seamlessly integrate sophisticated, magazine-quality text overlays using our brand fonts (Playfair Display, Butler) to elevate standard affiliate content.



Creative Direction: Help set the visual standard for the agency. When we scale and hire junior editors, your work will be the gold standard they follow.



[REQUIREMENTS]



The Software: You must be an expert in Adobe Premiere Pro and/or After Effects. (While these are for TikTok/IG, this role requires desktop-level precision, layering, and grading that mobile apps cannot provide).



The Platform DNA: You deeply understand what works on TikTok Shop and Instagram right now, but you know how to elevate those trends visually so they look expensive.



The Eye: You understand the power of white space, cinematic pacing, and sophisticated design. "Busy," "cheesy," or overly aggressive edits will not work here.



Experience: A proven track record of editing for premium brands, luxury markets, or high-performing e-commerce/affiliate accounts.



[HOW TO APPLY]



Start your application with the word "GARDE" so I know you possess strong attention to detail.



Send a link to your portfolio. I only want to see your best vertical (9:16) work for TikTok or Instagram. Show me 3 examples of cinematic, high-end, or editorial video.



Answer this: How do you balance the need for a fast, attention-grabbing TikTok "hook" with a high-end, luxurious visual aesthetic?

title: Content Operations Specialist (Multi-Brand Portfolio)
description:
I am an entrepreneur, realtor, and the founder of the Made for More brand. I am looking for a tech-savvy, visually-gifted Content Operator to manage the social media presence for my family’s brand portfolio.



The Growth Path: We are starting this role as a part-time position (15-20 hours/week) to manage multiple social media accounts. As we prepare to launch my new tech startup this position will rapidly scale into a full-time role managing the expanded multi-brand portfolio.



Our funnels are already built. I need you to be the "engine" that fuels these funnels with high-end, editorial content, taking the day-to-day posting completely off my plate so I can focus on building the businesses.

Video Assembly: Take raw clips of m edit them into high-performing Reels/TikToks with "editorial" subtitles and pacing.



Multi-Account Management: Post and schedule content daily across Instagram,
, and TikTok.

 story.

 and a 
-year-old on Instagram, how would you change the caption or the "hook"?

title: Senior Customer Service Representative
description:
HVAC Supply is hiring a Senior Customer Service Representative to be a primary point of contact for our customers — handling everything from order entry and quoting to proactive account outreach and resolving service issues. This is a senior-level seat for someone who's done the job before, knows distribution, and can operate independently in a remote setting.

You'll work directly with customers, our sales team, and operations to make sure orders move smoothly, pricing is accurate, problems get solved fast, and accounts feel taken care of. Because this role spans inside sales, order management, and support, we're looking for someone who's comfortable wearing all three hats in a single day.

What you'll do



Take inbound calls,
s, and orders from customers — entering orders accurately and quoting pricing in our ERP

Proactively reach out to assigned accounts to check in, surface opportunities, and grow wallet share

Resolve service issues end-to-end: tracking, returns, backorders, billing questions, product substitutions

Partner with outside sales reps on shared accounts — keeping them in the loop on activity and flagging risks or opportunities

Work cross-functionally with purchasing, warehouse, and accounting to keep orders flowing and customers informed

Maintain clean records in the ERP and follow internal pricing/process standards

Help mentor junior CSRs informally and contribute to improving how the team works



What we're looking for



5+ years of customer service or inside sales experience, ideally in distribution, wholesale, or B2B

Strong ERP fluency — Advantive experience is a big plus; comfort with any distribution ERP (Epicor, Infor, NetSuite, Eclipse, Prophet 21, etc.) is welcome

Confidence on the phone and in writing — you can de-escalate, sell, and explain technical product details with equal ease

Sharp attention to detail with pricing, part numbers, and order accuracy

Self-directed — remote work means we need someone who manages their own day without hand-holding

Solid tech setup at home and comfort using multiple systems (ERP,
, chat, possibly CRM and eCommerce platforms)



Nice to have



Familiarity with our product categories

Experience supporting an eCommerce channel (Shopify or similar) alongside traditional orders

History training or mentoring other CSRs

title: Real Estate Closer | Convert Warm Leads into Contracts
description:
Hourly + Commission-Based Real Estate Closer | Convert Warm Leads into Contracts

Description:

We’re seeking a sharp, commission-based Real Estate Closer to join our growing acquisitions team. This is not a cold calling role—you’ll be working exclusively with warm and hot leads already in our pipeline. Your only focus is turning motivated seller conversations into signed wholesale contracts.

We close 4+ deals per month and are scaling. You’ll be walking into a proven system, not trying to build one from scratch. Our team handles lead generation, follow-up, and transaction coordination. We just need a closer who can lock in the deals.

________________________________________

About Us:

We are an experienced real estate investment company working primarily in North Carolina. We specialize in off-market wholesale deals, creative financing, and helping sellers find quick, stress-free solutions. With a reliable marketing engine and a team handling systems, follow-up, and transaction coordination, you’ll be backed by solid infrastructure that allows you to focus on closing.

We consistently provide a steady flow of qualified leads ready for conversion and are committed to building long-term partnerships with our team.

________________________________________

Your Role:

• Speak with warm/hot seller leads by phone

• Build rapport, uncover motivation, and negotiate win-win offers

• Confidently guide the conversation toward a signed contract

• Use our CRM (GHL-based) to track notes and progress

• Submit signed contracts to the Transaction Coordinator for processing

• Follow simple SOPs and scripts

• Build out SoPs and training that mimic your role 

________________________________________

Ideal Candidate:

• 2+ years of experience as a real estate closer (wholesale preferred)

• Strong sales skills with a proven track record of converting leads into contracts

• Confident, clear communicator who can build trust quickly

• Highly organized and able to work independently

• Familiar with tools like GHL, Zoom, and Google Workspace

title: # Part-Time Marketing VA — Meta Ads & Client Success Manager
description:
## About DVRG



DVRG is a Tacoma-based marketing agency that produces high-impact video content, Meta ad creative, and lead generation systems for service-based businesses — contractors, remodelers, painters, and home service operators across the Pacific Northwest.



We run lean, move fast, and hold our work to a high standard. We're not a big agency with layers of management. We're a focused operation that takes pride in results and creative quality.



We're looking for a part-time VA to take the operational side of our campaigns off the founder's plate so he can stay in sales and strategy.



---



## The Role



This is a **10-hour per week, part-time remote position** — roughly 3 hours on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. Work is async-friendly, but we ask for at least a 1-hour overlap with Pacific Standard Time (PST) during morning hours for a weekly check-in.



You'll work directly with the founder across three core areas:



**1. Campaign Management & Reporting**

- Pull weekly Meta Ads performance reports across 6 active client accounts

- Monitor campaigns for underperformance (high CPL, low CTR, ad fatigue)

- Flag issues and log updates into our internal Slack tracker

- Upload approved creative and build out ad sets in Meta Business Suite

- Build and QA Meta Instant Forms



**2. Client Success Support**

- Send weekly performance recap messages to clients via Slack

- Follow up on missing assets, deliverables, or pending approvals

- Keep client communication organized and nothing falling through the cracks



**3. Operations & Admin**

- Organize Google Drive folders by client and date

- Format finalized ad scripts into our approved document template

- Maintain our ad swipe file with hooks and winning creative

- Research competitor ads via Meta Ad Library for client niches

- Support Zapier automation monitoring (flag broken zaps)



---



## You're a Fit If You:



- Have 2+ years of experience in a digital marketing agency or as a marketing VA

- Have hands-on experience inside Meta Business Suite / Ads Manager on real client accounts

- Know what CPL, CTR, ROAS, and ad frequency mean — no explanation needed

- Have worked with US-based clients before

- Are comfortable in Slack, Google Drive, and async communication

- Are detail-oriented, proactive, and flag problems before they become issues

- Can work independently without hand-holding on standard tasks



---



## Tools We Use



Meta Business Suite · Slack · Google Drive · Google Docs · Zapier · Notion



---



## What This Is NOT



- This is not a content creation or social media scheduling role

- This is not an entry-level position — we need someone who can hit the ground running

- This is not a full-time role (for now) — but there is room to grow as we scale



---



## Compensation & Hours



- **Rate:** $8 USD/hr

- **Hours:** ~10 hrs/week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday — ~3 hrs each)

- **Schedule:** Async with 1-hour PST overlap window preferred

- **Start:** ASAP



---



## How to Apply



Send a message with:



1. Your resume or a link to your OnlineJobs profile

2. A 2–3 sentence summary of your Meta Ads experience (be specific — what did you manage, what was the monthly ad spend, how many accounts?)

3. Any tools from our stack you've used before

4. Your availability and timezone



**Applications without Meta Ads experience will not be considered.**



We respond to every qualified application within 48 hours.

title: Sales & Tech Representative
description:
We’re Hiring!



Are you a skilled salesperson with a background in gas plumbing and/or electrical work? Do you enjoy helping customers find the right solution while using your technical knowledge? Join our growing team and work with a US online home goods retailer that values expertise, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.





Position:

Sales & Tech Representative – Technical Products (Gas & Electrical Focus)

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST (40 hours/week)

Salary: Competitive + Performance Bonuses





About the Role:

As a Salesperson, you will assist customers with product selection, provide technical support during the sales process, and close sales with confidence. You’ll work with gas fireplaces, heaters, stoves, and other appliances, so previous hands-on experience or technical knowledge of gas or electrical systems is a MUST.





Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Receive incoming phone calls and live chats from potential customers (prospects) who indicated that they need shopping assistance. This does not include calls and chats from existing customers that need help with their orders (that’s a job for a different department)

Help customers choose the right gas or electric heating appliance for their needs

Makes follow-up calls, writes follow-up
s, and text messages to potential buyers. We try to follow up 5x with each person that contacted us (unless they indicate that they are no longer interested). We don’t do cold calling. Only reaching out to prospects who interacted with our online store and left their contact information.

After each interaction with a prospect/customer, writes notes & comments in our CRM (Asana)

Communicate efficiently and build rapport with potential customers

Assists prospects in their shopping journey and helps make the purchase decision

Ensures that all prospects’ questions and objections are addressed

Leads every interaction to a close sale, which means an order is placed on our online store

Prepares custom quotes, price matches competitors if necessary, and offers custom discounts. This requires calculating margins & ensuring profitability.

Answering questions about the products we offer, including technical questions. Learns the specifications, features and benefits of our bestsellers. Over time, becomes an expert in the industry.

Explain technical specifications and installation requirements in simple terms

If not able to answer product-specific questions, conducts research in the materials provided by the supplier, or reaches out to the supplier to get the information

Confirms stock availability and lead times when offering products to prospects

Documents standard procedures and best practices in a written form, so that other tea
mbers can learn from them





Requirements:

Sales Experience – 2+ years, preferably with B2C or B2B technical products

Gas Plumbing or Electrical Experience – Formal training or work experience (installer, technician, or similar roles preferred)

Excellent English – Both written and spoken

Confident Communicator – Can explain complex concepts to non-technical customers

Tech-Savvy – Comfortable using CRMs,
 platforms, order tools, etc.

Reliable and Proactive – Takes initiative and works independently

Home Office Setup – Stable internet, noise-free environment, backup power is a plus





Preferred but Not Required:

Knowledge of venting, HVAC, or hearth appliances

US product or code familiarity

Experience in the construction or home improvement industry





Why Join Us?

Sales commission: 8% of gross profit from all orders closed. Gross profit means the sale amount minus the product and shipping cost. Paid monthly. No upper limit. Monthly commission payouts range between $1,200 (the guaranteed minimum) and as high as $3,000 (for the top performer). The average monthly payout is $1,465 (average from the last 2 years).

$1,200/month minimum guaranteed pay

15 days paid days off per year (also convertible to min. guaranteed commission)

Paid training on all aspects of the role

Opportunities for professional development

Friendly atmosphere

Online Interview process

Fully remote position





How to Apply:

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph with the subject line: "Sales Pro - [Your Name]"

title: Shopify Product Listing Virtual Assistant
description:

Responsibilities

• Rewrite product titles and descriptions (short, clean, not hype)

• Remove supplier branding or watermarks

• Follow a consistent product page structure



What You Will NOT Do



• No use of false urgency or misleading scarcity tactics

• No creation or use of fake reviews

• No unprofessional or deceptive visual edits

• No unsupported medical or health claims

• No low-quality or irrelevant product images



Our focus is clean, professional, and straightforward execution.



Product Image Rules



Each product must include:



• Clean product image

• Close-up detail

• Lifestyle image

• Benefit image (minimal text overlay)



All images must look:

• Branded

• High quality

• Not like AliExpress



You will use Nano Banana to improve competitor images and create better versions.



Product Page Structure



Each product page will include:



• Clean title (Brand + Keywords)

• Short intro paragraph

• 1–3 standard trust sections (same on all products)

• Related collection



Pay



$4–6/hour depending on experience.



If performance is strong, long term position available.



When applying, please begin your application with the keyword: Strawberry



Requirements



• Experience with Shopify product listing uploads

• Experience with Kopy (importing app) A MUST

• Basic image editing knowledge

• Can follow instructions exactly

• Fast and detail-oriented

• Good English

title: $8,000 From ONE Partnership - Need Crypto Network, Not Experience (Remote BD)
description:

Performance Bonus: Every 5 successful partnership you close triggers an immediate unlock of 20% your vested tokens. This allows you to bypass the 18-month vesting period and access your earnings as soon as you deliver results.

Note: A "successful partnership" is defined by the project signing the LP Bonds integration agreement and going live on our marketplace.

YOU HAVE:

-
/
 relationships with project founders or marketing heads

YOU DON’T NEED:

THE ROLE:

 handle

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (DeFi and NFT Projects)
description:
About Us

LP Bonds. Live DeFi protocol. 8+ chains. 50+ pools. Real users. Real TVL. We built this with a team most startups would call "impossible." We're not looking for someone who's done 10 years of safe corporate blockchain work. We're looking for someone who's hungry enough to own an entire protocol's technical stack and sharp enough to not break it.



THE ROLE

 You own everything technical. Smart contracts. Frontend. Backend. New chain deployments. DEX integrations. Infrastructure. There's no senior engineer above you to review your code. There's no architect drawing diagrams for you. You are the engineering department.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Solidity & Contracts:

- Deploy and manage production smart contracts across EVM chains - Bond NFTs, LP vaults, yield engines, upgrade systems, marketplace

- Ship new chain deployments end-to-end. We hand you a chain, you hand us live contracts.

- Integrate with DEX protocols (Camelot, PancakeSwap, Uniswap) at the contract level

- Security is your religion. You think about how things break before you think about how they work.



Frontend:

- React/Next.js - build and maintain the full user-facing application

- Web3 wallet integrations, stats dashboard, Bond NFT marketplace

- If the UI is broken, it's your problem. If it's slow, it's your problem.



Backend & Infra:

- Node.js API, MongoDB, IPFS

- Multi-chain indexing and event monitoring

- Keep 8+ chain deployments healthy simultaneously



WHO YOU ARE:

- You've written Solidity that's been deployed to mainnet. Even once. Even small. You've felt the weight of real money in your contract.

- You can build a full-stack app alone. Not "I contributed to a team project" - you built it.

- You learn by doing, not by asking. You hit a wall, you Google it, you read the docs, you figure it out, you ship.

- You're not waiting for permission. You see something broken, you fix it. You see something missing, you build it.

- You're young, hungry, and slightly terrified of how much responsibility this is. Good. That fear keeps you sharp.





WE CARE ABOUT:

- What you've shipped. Show us. Links or it didn't happen.

- How fast you learn when thrown into something you've never done

- Whether you take ownership or make excuses

- Whether you stay up until 3am because the bug is bothering you, not because someone told you to



This is a small team building something most of DeFi hasn't figured out yet. You will be stretched. You will ship things you didn't think you could ship. You will learn more here than anywhere else because there's no one to hide behind.



If that excites you, apply. If it scares you, even better - apply anyway.

title: Product Development Assistant (Apparel)
description:
Job Title: Product Development Assistant (Apparel)

Salary: 50k PHP per month (depending on experience)



---



Company Description



Insomnia Visuals is a Los Angeles-based alternative streetwear and apparel brand with customers all over the world. We operate both DTC and wholesale, producing hundreds of styles per year. Our team is fast-paced, detail-oriented, and focused on building a globally recognized brand. We currently have tea
mbers across the U.S., Philippines, and Asia.



---



Job Summary



We’re looking for a highly organized, detail-oriented Product Development Assistant to support our product team across design, sampling, and production. This role is execution-heavy and requires someone who can track a high volume of styles, communicate with overseas factories, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks.



You will be working directly with our product, design, and supply chain teams to keep development moving efficiently across 1000+ styles per year.



THIS IS A FULL-TIME ROLE. If you are working multiple jobs or freelancing, this is NOT a fit. We are looking for someone long-term who wants to grow within the company.



---



Day-to-Day Tasks Include:



* Tracking styles from concept to sampling to production

* Maintaining and updating product data (materials, trims, measurements, costing)

* Communicating with manufacturers on samples, revisions, and timelines

* Following up daily to ensure samples and production stay on schedule

* Managing sample tracking (what’s in development, shipped, approved, etc.)

* Assisting with tech pack organization, revisions, and comments

* Cross-checking product details to ensure accuracy before production

* Inputting and updating costing sheets and margin tracking

* Flagging delays, errors, or missing information proactively



---



MUST HAVE:



3+ years experience in apparel product development, sourcing, or production

MUST HAVE GRADUATED WITH A DEGREE FROM A FASHION SCHOOL

Experience working with overseas manufacturers (China preferred)

Strong understanding of tech packs, garments, and materials

HIGH attention to detail

Excellent written and spoken English

Experience with tools like Google Sheets / Excel

Comfortable working full-time and handling multiple styles at once



IF YOU DO NOT HAVE ALL OF THE ABOVE, DO NOT APPLY.



---



Qualifications:



* Reliable high-speed internet and a working webcam

* Able to work independently without constant supervision

* Highly organized and able to manage multiple workflows

* Strong communication and follow-up skills

* ON TIME, EVERY DAY



---



Nice to Have:



* Experience with Airtable or PLM systems

* Experience working with fashion brands

* Experience in high-SKU or fast-paced apparel environments

* Basic understanding of costing and margins



---



What We Offer:



Competitive salary with room to grow

Long-term, stable role within a growing company

A focused, high-performance team environment

Clear systems and processes



---



Application Process:
in the subject line of your application.



Submit:

1. Resume

2. A SHORT, CUSTOM cover letter explaining why you are a strong fit with specific examples of relevant work experience.

3. Your internet speed test screenshot



IF YOU SEND A GENERIC OR COPY-PASTED COVER LETTER OR MESSAGE TO ME, IT WILL BE DISCARDED. NO AI. IM ONLY LOOKING FOR SOMETHING SHORT AND SWEET TO TELL ME WHY YOU SHOULD GET THIS JOB. YOU DONT NEED AI TO WRITE THIS. 3-4 SENTENCES MAX.



We are looking for detail-oriented, serious applicants only. This is a critical role in the company.



If we like your application, we will reach out with a link to schedule an interview. We are looking to hire this NOW. for starting ASAP.

title: Fractional Finance Lead — Books Cleanup, Financial Model & Ongoing Advisory (High-Ticket Info Product / Coaching Busines
description:
Fractional Finance Lead — Books Cleanup, Financial Model & Ongoing Advisory

(High-Ticket Info Product / Coaching Business)



Phase 1 + 2: Fixed price | Phase 3: ~$1,200/month retainer | Long-term role



???? Note: This role requires working across US time zones with occasional EU overlap. Philippines-based applicants with experience in US-based online coaching businesses are strongly preferred.



HARD FILTER — READ BEFORE APPLYING

You must have direct hands-on experience working with high-ticket info product, coaching, or creator economy businesses. Not SaaS. Not e-commerce. Not traditional agency. Specifically the high-ticket online coaching world. If you've never built a financial model or cleaned books for a coaching or info product business doing $1M+ ARR, please do not apply.



ABOUT US

We're a high-ticket coaching and info product business in the creator economy doing $100K–$160K/month in revenue. We sell a tiered accelerator program (Accelerator ? VIP ? Elite), continuation offers, and a low-ticket community that funnels into high-ticket. We're scaling fast — but we're making every decision from gut feel and Slack panic instead of clean data. We need someone who can build the financial clarity we need to scale, then stay on as our fractional finance advisor.



YOU NEED TO ALREADY UNDERSTAND — WITHOUT US EXPLAINING

? Webinar ? application ? high-ticket sales funnel economics

? The cash flow gap between pay-in-full, external financing (Affirm/Klarna), and internal payment plans

? Processor holds, reserves, and chargeback reserves on Stripe and PayPal

? LTV mechanics across front-end and continuation/backend offers

? How to model a coaching team's capacity vs. revenue per coach

? The lag between hiring a sales, marketing, or coaching role and when they generate return



THE WORK — THREE PHASES



PHASE 1 — Books cleanup (one-time)

- Migrate from QuickBooks to Xero

- Reconcile and clean books from August 2025 to current

- Set up tracking categories to separate our coaching business from a small legacy video production line on the same entity

- Deliver a clean accrual P&L and cash flow showing actual historical profitability

- Accounts: Chase (primary), Bank of America (legacy), AmEx, Stripe, PayPal, Fundbases, WAP



PHASE 2 — Financial model build (one-time)

- Assumptions tab: LTV, customers per offer tier, PIF vs payment plan mix, processor fees, gross margin, continuation offer attach rate, low-ticket ? high-ticket conversion

- Headcount tab: plug in any new hire and immediately see P&L and cash flow impact, with realistic ramp time modelled before they generate return

- Overhead tab: software subscriptions and recurring fixed costs

- 2–3 year monthly P&L with quarterly and annual rollups

- Monthly cash flow (weekly during tight stretches)

- Clean enough that a non-finance founder can toggle assumptions and instantly see the impact



PHASE 3 — Ongoing fractional advisor (~$1,200/month)

- Maintain and update the model as the business changes

- Advise on hiring, pricing, offer structure, and scaling decisions

- Regular working sessions (monthly or bi-weekly cadence TBD)

- Coordinate with our US-based tax preparer at year-end

- Available for ad-hoc strategic finance questions



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN 90 DAYS

? Clean Xero books from August 2025 to current

? We know exactly how profitable we've been, month by month

? A working financial model driving every hiring and scaling decision

? Zero decisions from gut feel — everything comes from data



COMPENSATION

- Phase 1: Propose your fixed price

- Phase 2: Propose your fixed price

- Phase 3: ~$1,200/month, grows with scope



HOW TO APPLY:

1. Send an
 to 
 (remove spaces) with the following:

Subject: Your Name - Years of Experience



2. Send a loom video: (IMPORTANT! No loom = no interview)

-  Name the high-ticket info product, coaching, or creator economy businesses you've worked with — specific client type and revenue range, not just "online businesses."

- Share a redacted or anonymized financial model for a coaching or info product business. Show us the assumptions tab, headcount logic, and how toggles flow through to P&L and cash flow.

- Propose your fixed price for Phase 1 and Phase 2, and your monthly rate for Phase 3.

- Your timezone and weekly availability.



? We shortlist based on relevance of past clients first, model quality second, price third.

title: UK Company Hiring: US Cold Callers
description:
Hiring  Cold callers to Help Build Local Business In the USA

 Base pay $600 per month (after two week training) plus commissions OTE each month $1000-$1500

Company: Start Over Movement Holdings Ltd (Founded by Sir Marco Robinson)

Location: Remote - 9:00 PM - 6:00 AM Philippines Time (8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Mississippi Central Time)

Type: Full-time Independent Contractor

---

The Story Behind Start Over

Sir Marco Robinson is a UK entrepreneur, author, and TV personality who has spent decades helping people rebuild their lives and businesses. He founded Start Over Movement with a simple idea: local business owners should support each other, not compete for the same scraps.

Every Start Over chapter is a community of local business owners who:

Refer clients to each other, generating real revenue

Learn marketing and sales directly from Sir Marco

Get a dedicated commission-only closer assigned to their business

Meet monthly with an exclusive group (only one business per industry per chapter)

title: Google Ads Manager & Meta Marketing Manager
description:
Google Ads Account Manager (Part-Time, Remote)



About the Company

HouseSensationsArt is a U.S.-based e-commerce and manufacturing brand specializing in custo
tal art, signage, and personalized home décor products.

We are growing our digital marketing team and are looking for a Google Ads Account Manager to support and scale our paid advertising efforts.



About the Role

We are hiring a Google Ads Account Manager to manage and grow three Google Ads accounts.

One existing account requires a full rebuild due to website and SEO updates

Two accounts are new and will require full setup and campaign launches

This is a long-term role for the right candidate who can manage performance-driven campaigns and work independently.



Responsibilities

Set up, rebuild, and manage Google Ads campaigns

Write ad copy and create basic ad creatives (graphics)

Optimize campaigns for conversions, CTR, and ROI

Set up GA4, Google Tag Manager, and conversion tracking (including Stape if needed)

Build and maintain Google Looker Studio reports

Monitor performance and provide weekly updates

Collaborate with the team on campaign strategy improvements



Requirements

Google Ads Certified (required)

Proven experience managing Google Ads campaigns

Strong understanding of conversion tracking (GA4, GTM, Looker Studio)

Experience with ad copywriting

Basic graphic design skills (Canva or similar tools)

Detail-oriented, reliable, and able to work independently

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills



Contract Details

Hours: 20 hours/week

 Schedule: 10:00 PM–2:00 AM Manila Time

 Work Days: Monday–Friday (USA and PH)

 Work Setup: Remote (Philippines only)

 Pay: $3.00/hour, paid weekly via Wise

 Salary Review: After 90 days

 Requirements: NDA, contract agreement, TimeProof tracking, and daily timesheet



How to Apply

Send your application to: 

Include the following:

Link to your OnlineJobs.ph profile (Philippines applicants only)

Updated resume

Portfolio (if available)

Google Ads certification proof

A brief introduction about yourself and relevant experience

Your availability and earliest start date

Confirmation that you agree to:

Pay rate

Work hours

NDA and contract

Time tracking and reporting requirements



Important

Applications that do not follow all instructions will not be considered.

title: Short-Form Editor & Designer
description:
About Breaklabs

Breaklabs is a growing sports card breaking channel on Whatnot running live rips of NBA, NFL, MLB, and Pokémon products. We're obsessed with running clean, hype-driven shows — great camera work, fair break formats, and real relationships with our regulars. We're looking for a video editor with strong graphic design chops who lives and breathes the hobby to help us turn Breaklabs into a destination channel.



The Role

You'll own the visual side of everything that happens outside the live show — the cuts, clips, and graphics that get people to click "Join Show" and remember the channel. This is a hands-on creator role, not a render-and-deliver job. If you can't talk comfortably about PSA pop reports, Bowman 1sts, Prizm color tiers, or National Treasures RPAs, you'll miss the moments worth clipping.



What You'll Do

Cut show footage into short-form content (pulls, reactions, funny moments, customer hits) and get it posted within hours of the break, not days — for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

Edit longer recap content for YouTube — top 5 pulls of the week, show highlights, breaker reactions

Design graphics and break announcements in Canva or Figma — show schedules, featured product drops, giveaway posts, winner callouts, channel art, thumbnail templates

Build a consistent visual identity across thumbnails, overlays, and graphics so Breaklabs looks like Breaklabs from the first frame

Create motion graphics, lower thirds, and on-screen elements for live shows when needed (countdown timers, hit alerts, sponsor bumpers)

Maintain a tight clip library and naming convention so nothing gets lost

Coordinate with the on-camera team on what's worth clipping, what graphics are needed for upcoming shows, and turnaround priorities

Iterate on what's working — track which clips and thumbnails drive the most Whatnot follows and show attendance, then make more of that



What You Bring

Deep familiarity with the sports card hobby — you know the products, the rip culture, and the personalities. Whatnot watching history is a big plus

Strong video editing chops in CapCut, Premiere, or DaVinci — fast turnaround matters more than cinematic polish (send us a reel)

Graphic design ability in Canva or Figma — show us your work

A good eye for pull moments worth clipping and short-form hooks that stop the scroll

Experience designing thumbnails that earn the click (not just the impression)

Self-directed — you'll get direction, not hand-holding



Nice to Have

After Effects experience for motion graphics

Experience editing for another breaker, card shop, or sports content channel

Sound design instincts — knowing when to lean into hype audio vs. let a pull breathe

Your own collection (show it off in your application)

title: Military Content Scriptwriter & Footage Researcher - Short Form & Long Form Video
description:
US focused military content page with 2.4 million followers on
. We publish short form video reels daily and are expanding into long form documentary content covering military operations, weapons systems, equipment, and history. All footage is sourced from public domain military archives. We need a dedicated scriptwriter and footage researcher to own the quality and retention of everything we produce.



Footage Sourcing



Search public domain military archives daily for the strongest video candidates across short form and long form formats

Assess each candidate for hook potential, visual variety, and story arc before recommending it

Deliver footage links to the editor with a one-line brief explaining the strongest angle and why it will perform

Stay ahead of the content calendar — footage must be identified the evening before production begins

Build a running library of strong footage organised by category — vehicles, aviation, naval, special operations, interdictions, historical



Short Form Scriptwriting



Write three to five short form VO scripts per day for reels between thirty and sixty seconds

Apply a structured story framework to every script without exception — strong hook, open loop, midway hook at fifteen to twenty seconds, and a payoff that rewards patience

Format all scripts for ElevenLabs AI voiceover — two to three words per line, numbers as words, specific safe language substitutions applied throughout

Deliver finished scripts with hook identified, midway hook timecoded, payoff marked, stat card suggestions included, and ElevenLabs pacing notes for the voice artist



Long Form Scriptwriting



Write 4-5 long form documentary scripts per week between three and ten minutes in length

Structure each script around a full narrative arc — origin, conflict, escalation, resolution, legacy

Layer facts and human stakes together — long form content must feel like a documentary, not a Wikipedia article read aloud

Identify the strongest retention spikes within the long form structure and engineer deliberate hooks at the two minute, four minute, and six minute marks to prevent drop-off

Deliver long form scripts with full timecode guidance, section headers for the editor, and music energy notes throughout



Editor Collaboration



Work directly with the editor daily via Slack — scripts and footage delivered together so he has everything he needs without chasing

Review rough cuts and flag any VO timing mismatches before final export

Suggest stat card text, music energy notes, and visual pacing guidance alongside every script

Be available during editing hours to answer questions and turn around quick revisions fast



Retention Ownership



Track retention performance on every published piece of content

Identify which hook formulas, story spines, and VO styles are driving the highest watch time

Bring weekly retention data into your scripting decisions — what worked last week should inform what you write this week

Flag underperforming content patterns and propose structural fixes

title: Join Our Team as a Viral UGC Clips Creator (Short-Form + AI Videos)

We’re looking for a Viral UGC Clips Creator & Video Editor who knows how to turn raw footage into high-retention, scroll-stopping clips for social media and can also create high-quality, realistic AI-generated ad videos.



This role is not about basic editing — it’s about understanding virality, hooks, pacing, storytelling, and leveraging AI tools to produce compelling ad creatives.



We’re Looking For



A creative who:

Understands what makes short-form videos go viral

Can identify strong hooks, moments, and emotional beats from raw footage

Knows how to edit for retention, watch time, and replays

Is familiar with trends, formats, and platform-native styles

Can create realistic AI ad videos (UGC-style, avatars, voiceovers, etc.)

Can consistently produce multiple high-quality clips per week

Understands internet culture, short-form psychology, and AI-driven content creation.



What You’ll Do

Raw testimonial videos

Product demos

Creator footage

Existing long-form content

Realistic AI UGC-style videos

AI avatars or voiceovers

AI-enhanced storytelling and visuals



Add:

Strong opening hooks (first 1–3 seconds matter most)

On-screen captions optimized for retention

Fast pacing, jump cuts, zooms, and pattern interrupts

Repurpose clips across social media platforms

Study what’s already working and iterate fast



You Bring



Proven experience editing short-form videos that drive:

High watch time

Strong engagement

Shares or saves

Experience creating high-quality, realistic AI ad videos

A strong sense of:

Hook writing

Story flow

Visual rhythm

Familiarity with tools like:

CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or similar

AI tools (e.g., Heygen, Runway, Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, or similar)

Ability to follow brand guidelines while keeping content native and authentic

Comfort working quickly and iterating based on feedback



To Apply

A brief explanation of why you think those clips performed well

Examples of AI-generated ad videos you’ve created



Additionally, please record and send a short Loom video answering the following questions (APPLICATIONS WITHOUT A LOOM WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED):



What is your process for creating a high-performing short-form ad from raw footage?

Which AI tools have you used to create ad creatives, and how do you ensure they look realistic and engaging?

We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Virtual Assistant
description:
# AI-Proficient Virtual Assistant for AI Automation Consultancy (Part-Time, 10–20 hrs/week)



## About Praixis Labs

Praixis Labs is a fast-growing AI automation and workflow consultancy based in Austin, Texas. We help small and medium-sized businesses save time and money by building custom AI-powered workflows using tools like Claude, n8n, and Notion.



I'm the founder and currently a one-person operation — and that's exactly the problem. Client work is picking up, and I need a sharp, reliable VA to take the operational load off my plate so I can focus on building automations and growing the business.



## Who I'm Looking For

You're someone who:

- **Works fluently with AI tools** — especially Claude (Projects, Artifacts, MCP). You don't just "use ChatGPT sometimes." You actually think with AI as a daily collaborator.

- **Is detail-obsessed about CRM hygiene.** A messy pipeline drives you nuts. You'll keep contacts, deals, and follow-ups clean and current.

- **Communicates clearly in written English.** You'll be drafting follow-ups, social posts, and internal updates.

- **Is proactive, not reactive.** I want someone who flags "hey, you haven't followed up with this lead in 10 days" — not someone waiting to be told.

- **Is comfortable in a remote, async setup.** Most communication will be via Slack/Loom/email.



## Core Responsibilities

- **CRM management (HubSpot):** Logging interactions, updating deal stages, scheduling follow-ups, list segmentation, data cleanup.

- **Calendar & scheduling:** Booking discovery calls, prep notes for meetings, time-zone coordination.

- **Job posts & hiring ops:** Drafting and posting roles, screening applicants, scheduling intro calls.

- **Social media support:** Drafting LinkedIn posts (in my voice), scheduling, tracking engagement.

- **General executive assistant work:** Inbox triage, research, light project coordination, document formatting.



## Tools You Should Know (or Be Eager to Learn Fast)

**Required:**

- Claude (Projects, Artifacts, ideally MCP)

- HubSpot or comparable CRM (Pipedrive, Salesforce, etc.)

- Google Workspace (Calendar, Gmail, Drive, Docs)

- Slack



**Strongly preferred:**

- n8n (or similar — Make, Zapier)

- Jira or Linear

- LinkedIn (posting, engagement, Sales Navigator a plus)

- Notion (we run our CRM and ops in it)



If you don't know a tool but you can show me you learn fast with AI, that counts.



## The Setup

- **Hours:** 10–20 hours/week to start, with room to grow as the business does.

- **Rate:** $10–20/hr depending on experience and tool fluency.

- **Schedule:** Some overlap with US Central Time required (at least 2–3 hours/day).

- **Long-term:** I'm hiring for a real working relationship, not a one-off gig. The right person grows with the company.



## How to Apply

Please send a short message that includes:



1. **Your relevant experience** — especially with HubSpot, Claude, and any automation tools.

2. **A short Loom video (2–3 minutes)** walking me through one workflow or system you've built or managed for a previous client. Bonus points if AI was involved.

3. **The phrase "Praixis pilot"** at the top of your message so I know you actually read this post.

4. **Your weekly availability and rate.**



Applications without all four will be skipped — not because I'm rigid, but because attention to detail is the job.



Looking forward to meeting you.



— Ivan

Praixis Labs

title: Technical Operations Specialist (GHL & Digital Assets)
description:
Systems-Focused GHL Specialist for Boutique Marketing Agency



About the Role:

We are a specialized growth agency looking for a self-reliant, tech-forward GHL Specialist to serve as our technical partner. We are currently in a "Strategic Build" phase and are seeking a long-term collaborator to architect, document, and refine our proprietary systems as we move toward full-scale operations.



The Partnership Path:

- Phase 1 (Trial/Build): Project-based work to establish core "Elite" workflows, high-logic pipelines, and landing page templates.

- Phase 2 (Growth): Regular weekly touch-base meetings as we onboard initial clients and monitor system health. *Part Time

- Phase 3 (Scale): Ongoing technical management, data analysis, and system optimization. *Possibility to add hours or shift to Full Time



Key Responsibilities:

- GHL Architecture: Build, audit, and manage sub-account Snapshots, including nested if/else workflows, complex pipelines, and multi-step custom forms.

- Web & Landing Pages: Build high-converting landing pages and agency sites directly within the GHL funnel builder.

- Technical Logistics: Manage A2P 10DLC registrations, domain integrations, and administrative access protocols.

- Fulfillment Support: Assist with Google Business Profile (GBP) management and technical administrative setups.

- Reporting & Data Analysis: Weekly extraction of KPIs from GHL and PPC dashboards. You will prepare data summaries (lead volume, tier-ranking, and cost-per-lead) to assist the founder in client reporting.

- The System Log: Maintain a detailed, client-by-client technical log (SOP) documenting every automation, tagging logic, and pipeline structure. You must be able to explain the logic behind every build.

- System Optimization: Proactively suggest GHL features or third-party integrations (Zapier, etc.) that could improve client ROI.



Bonus Skills:

- Experience or familiarity with white-label Google Ads management or major white-label PPC providers.



Who You Are:

- Tech-Forward: You stay updated on the latest GHL BETA releases and use modern integrations.

- Self-Reliant: You take ownership of a task from start to finish and solve problems proactively.

- Articulate: You can explain complex logic clearly and concisely in plain English.

- Positive & Professional: You enjoy building systems from the ground up and value long-term partnership.



To Apply:

Please provide a brief video (Loom, Zoom, or similar) or a detailed screen-share presentation showing:

1. A complex GHL workflow or landing page you have built (specifically showing if/else logic).

2. An example of how you document your technical work/SOPs for a founder or client.

3. Your favorite tech integration or GHL feature that most people overlook.

4. A bit about you and why we should work together!
